You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_16.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 16: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[16]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_16.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 16: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	Playwright visual audit: citation collapse/re-expand with a LONG scrollable report.
3	
4	Previous tests used short reports that fit in the viewport. scroll_sync's
5	IntersectionObserver never fired because all headings were visible at once.
6	This test injects a 69-citation, 8-section report spanning many viewports so
7	that scroll_sync actively swaps citation cards as the user scrolls -- exposing
8	the real bug path.
9	
10	Usage:
11	    python scripts/playwright_cite_collapse_long.py
12	
13	Outputs screenshots to outputs/cite_collapse_long/
14	"""
15	
16	import asyncio
17	import json
18	import os
19	import socket
20	import subprocess
21	import sys
22	import time
23	from pathlib import Path
24	
25	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	# Configuration (LAW VI: no hard-coded values in logic)
27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
29	SCREENSHOT_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "outputs" / "cite_collapse_long"
30	SERVER_SCRIPT = PROJECT_ROOT / "scripts" / "live_server.py"
31	SERVER_LOG = PROJECT_ROOT / "logs" / "cite_collapse_long_server.log"
32	
33	PAGE_LOAD_WAIT_MS = 3000
34	JS_INIT_WAIT_MS = 1500
35	SCROLL_SETTLE_MS = 800
36	COLLAPSE_SETTLE_MS = 300
37	EXPAND_SETTLE_MS = 800
38	ACCORDION_SETTLE_MS = 300
39	
40	
41	def _find_free_port() -> int:
42	    """Bind to port 0 and let the OS assign a free port."""
43	    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
44	        s.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
45	        return s.getsockname()[1]
46	
47	
48	def _wait_for_server(port: int, timeout: float = 15.0) -> bool:
49	    """Poll until the server accepts TCP connections."""
50	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
51	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
52	        try:
53	            with socket.create_connection(("127.0.0.1", port), timeout=1.0):
54	                return True
55	        except OSError:
56	            time.sleep(0.3)
57	    return False
58	
59	
60	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	# JavaScript: inject a LONG multi-section report with 20 bibliography entries
62	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	JS_INJECT_LONG_REPORT = """
65	() => {
66	    // 1. Build bibliography with 20 entries
67	    state.bibliography = [];
68	    for (var i = 1; i <= 20; i++) {
69	        state.bibliography.push({
70	            url: "https://source" + i + ".example.com/article",
71	            title: "Research Source " + i + " — Long Title About Water Treatment Technology and Filtration Methods",
72	            domain: "source" + i + ".example.com",
73	            is_faithful: i <= 16,
74	            snippet: "This is the snippet for source " + i + " discussing findings about water treatment."
75	        });
76	    }
77	
78	    // 2. Switch to report phase
79	    setWorkspacePhase("report");
80	
81	    // 3. Build a LONG report with 8 sections, each with different citations
82	    //    and lots of filler text to force scrolling past viewport
83	    var sections = [];
84	    for (var s = 1; s <= 8; s++) {
85	        var sec = "## Section " + s + ": Research Area " + s + "\\n\\n";
86	        // 6 paragraphs per section to make it long enough to scroll
87	        for (var p = 0; p < 6; p++) {
88	            sec += "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. " +
89	                "Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. " +
90	                "Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris. " +
91	                "Duis aute irure dolor in reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum. ";
92	            // Sprinkle citations from this section's range
93	            var citeA = ((s - 1) * 2 + 1);
94	            var citeB = ((s - 1) * 2 + 2);
95	            if (citeB > 20) citeB = 20;
96	            sec += "[" + citeA + "] and [" + citeB + "]. ";
97	            sec += "Excepteur sint occaecat cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollit anim id est laborum. ";
98	        }
99	        sec += "\\n\\n";
100	        sections.push(sec);
101	    }
102	
103	    var fullReport = sections.join("");
104	    appendReportBlock(fullReport, state.bibliography);
105	    _updateCitationsCount();
106	
107	    return {
108	        bib_length: state.bibliography.length,
109	        report_length: fullReport.length,
110	        sections_count: sections.length
111	    };
112	}
113	"""
114	
115	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	# JavaScript: comprehensive DOM inspection at each step
117	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
118	
119	JS_INSPECT_DOM = """
120	() => {
121	    var cards = document.querySelectorAll('.ws-cite-card');
122	    var citeNums = [];
123	    cards.forEach(function(c) {
124	        var n = c.getAttribute('data-cite-num');
125	        if (n) citeNums.push(parseInt(n));
126	    });
127	
128	    var citeList = document.getElementById('ws-citation-list');
129	    var citeSec = document.getElementById('ws-section-citations');
130	    var sectionBody = citeSec ? citeSec.querySelector('.ws-section-body') : null;
131	    var badge = document.getElementById('ws-citations-count');
132	
133	    // Compute section classes
134	    var sectionClasses = '';
135	    if (citeSec) {
136	        sectionClasses = citeSec.className;
137	    }
138	
139	    // Computed styles
140	    var citeListDisplay = 'N/A';
141	    var sectionBodyDisplay = 'N/A';
142	    if (citeList) {
143	        citeListDisplay = window.getComputedStyle(citeList).display;
144	    }
145	    if (sectionBody) {
146	        sectionBodyDisplay = window.getComputedStyle(sectionBody).display;
147	    }
148	
149	    return {
150	        card_count: cards.length,
151	        cite_nums: citeNums,
152	        inner_html_length: citeList ? citeList.innerHTML.length : -1,
153	        inner_html_first200: citeList ? citeList.innerHTML.substring(0, 200) : 'NOT_FOUND',
154	        badge_text: badge ? badge.textContent.trim() : 'NOT_FOUND',
155	        section_classes: sectionClasses,
156	        cite_list_display: citeListDisplay,
157	        section_body_display: sectionBodyDisplay,
158	        phase: typeof _wsPhase !== 'undefined' ? _wsPhase : 'unknown',
159	        observer_exists: typeof _scrollSyncObserver !== 'undefined' && _scrollSyncObserver !== null,
160	        active_heading_id: typeof _scrollSyncActiveHeadingId !== 'undefined' ? _scrollSyncActiveHeadingId : null
161	    };
162	}
163	"""
164	
165	
166	def _print_dom_state(label: str, data: dict) -> None:
167	    """Pretty-print DOM inspection data."""
168	    print(f"\n  --- DOM State: {label} ---")
169	    print(f"  card_count:          {data['card_count']}")
170	    print(f"  cite_nums:           {data['cite_nums']}")
171	    print(f"  innerHTML length:    {data['inner_html_length']}")
172	    print(f"  innerHTML[0:200]:    {data['inner_html_first200'][:200]}")
173	    print(f"  badge_text:          {data['badge_text']}")
174	    print(f"  section_classes:     {data['section_classes']}")
175	    print(f"  cite_list_display:   {data['cite_list_display']}")
176	    print(f"  section_body_display:{data['section_body_display']}")
177	    print(f"  phase:               {data['phase']}")
178	    print(f"  observer_exists:     {data['observer_exists']}")
179	    print(f"  active_heading_id:   {data['active_heading_id']}")
180	    print(f"  ---")
181	
182	
183	async def run_audit():
184	    """Main audit function."""
185	    from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
186	
187	    SCREENSHOT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
188	
189	    # Find a free port
190	    port = _find_free_port()
191	    url = f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}"
192	    print(f"[AUDIT] Using port {port}, URL: {url}")
193	
194	    # Start the live server with stdout redirected to log file (Windows deadlock fix)
195	    SERVER_LOG.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
196	    log_fh = open(str(SERVER_LOG), "w", encoding="utf-8")
197	    server_proc = subprocess.Popen(
198	        [
199	            sys.executable, str(SERVER_SCRIPT),
200	            "--no-tunnel",
201	            "--port", str(port),
202	        ],
203	        stdout=log_fh,
204	        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
205	        cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
206	    )
207	    print(f"[AUDIT] Server PID={server_proc.pid}, log={SERVER_LOG}")
208	
209	    try:
210	        # Wait for server to be ready
211	        if not _wait_for_server(port, timeout=15.0):
212	            print("[AUDIT] FATAL: Server did not start within 15 seconds.")
213	            return
214	
215	        print("[AUDIT] Server is up. Launching Playwright...")
216	
217	        async with async_playwright() as pw:
218	            browser = await pw.chromium.launch(headless=True)
219	            context = await browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 1440, "height": 900})
220	            page = await context.new_page()
221	
222	            # Collect console messages for debugging
223	            console_errors = []
224	            page.on(
225	                "console",
226	                lambda msg: console_errors.append(f"[{msg.type}] {msg.text}")
227	                if msg.type in ("error", "warning")
228	                else None,
229	            )
230	
231	            # Navigate with domcontentloaded (NOT networkidle -- SSE keeps conn open)
232	            print(f"[AUDIT] Navigating to {url} ...")
233	            await page.goto(url, wait_until="domcontentloaded", timeout=15000)
234	
235	            # Wait for JS init
236	            await page.wait_for_timeout(PAGE_LOAD_WAIT_MS)
237	            print("[AUDIT] Page loaded. Injecting LONG report state...")
238	
239	            # Inject state with LONG report
240	            inject_result = await page.evaluate(JS_INJECT_LONG_REPORT)
241	            print(f"[AUDIT] Injection result: {inject_result}")
242	
243	            # Wait for scroll_sync to initialize (appendReportBlock has 100ms setTimeout)
244	            await page.wait_for_timeout(JS_INIT_WAIT_MS)
245	
246	            # ==================================================================
247	            # Results tracking
248	            # ==================================================================
249	            all_steps = {}
250	            pass_count = 0
251	            fail_count = 0
252	
253	            def check(step_name: str, key: str, value, expected_desc: str,
254	                       ok: bool) -> None:
255	                nonlocal pass_count, fail_count
256	                status = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
257	                if ok:
258	                    pass_count += 1
259	                else:
260	                    fail_count += 1
261	                all_steps.setdefault(step_name, [])
262	                all_steps[step_name].append({
263	                    "key": key, "value": value,
264	                    "expected": expected_desc, "status": status,
265	                })
266	                print(f"  [{status}] {step_name}.{key} = {value}  (expected: {expected_desc})")
267	
268	            # ==================================================================
269	            # Step 0: Initial state -- report at top, section 1 citations show
270	            # ==================================================================
271	            print("\n" + "=" * 70)
272	            print("STEP 0: Initial state")
273	            print("=" * 70)
274	
275	            dom0 = await page.evaluate(JS_INSPECT_DOM)
276	            _print_dom_state("step0", dom0)
277	
278	            check("step0", "card_count", dom0["card_count"], "> 0",
279	                  dom0["card_count"] > 0)
280	            check("step0", "badge_text", dom0["badge_text"], "20",
281	                  dom0["badge_text"] == "20")
282	            check("step0", "section_expanded", "expanded" in dom0["section_classes"],
283	                  "True (expanded)", "expanded" in dom0["section_classes"])
284	            check("step0", "phase", dom0["phase"], "report",
285	                  dom0["phase"] == "report")
286	            check("step0", "observer_exists", dom0["observer_exists"], "True",
287	                  dom0["observer_exists"] is True)
288	
289	            # Save step0 card data for later comparison
290	            step0_cite_nums = dom0["cite_nums"]
291	            step0_card_count = dom0["card_count"]
292	
293	            screenshot_path = str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "step0_initial.png")
294	            await page.screenshot(path=screenshot_path, full_page=False)
295	            print(f"  Screenshot saved: {screenshot_path}")
296	
297	            # ==================================================================
298	            # Step 1: Scroll to middle of report (section 4-5 area)
299	            #   scroll_sync should fire and change citation sidebar to different nums
300	            # ==================================================================
301	            print("\n" + "=" * 70)
302	            print("STEP 1: Scroll to middle of report (section 4)")
303	            print("=" * 70)
304	
305	            await page.evaluate("""
306	                () => {
307	                    var thread = document.getElementById('ws-thread');
308	                    thread.scrollTop = Math.floor(thread.scrollHeight / 2);
309	                }
310	            """)
311	            await page.wait_for_timeout(SCROLL_SETTLE_MS)
312	
313	            dom1 = await page.evaluate(JS_INSPECT_DOM)
314	            _print_dom_state("step1", dom1)
315	
316	            check("step1", "card_count", dom1["card_count"], "> 0",
317	                  dom1["card_count"] > 0)
318	            # The cite_nums should be DIFFERENT from step0 (scroll_sync is active)
319	            nums_changed = set(dom1["cite_nums"]) != set(step0_cite_nums)
320	            check("step1", "cite_nums_changed", nums_changed,
321	                  "True (different from step0, proving scroll_sync active)",
322	                  nums_changed)
323	            check("step1", "active_heading_changed",
324	                  dom1["active_heading_id"] is not None
325	                  and dom1["active_heading_id"] != dom0.get("active_heading_id"),
326	                  "True (heading tracker changed)",
327	                  dom1["active_heading_id"] is not None
328	                  and dom1["active_heading_id"] != dom0.get("active_heading_id"))
329	
330	            step1_cite_nums = dom1["cite_nums"]
331	            step1_card_count = dom1["card_count"]
332	            step1_html_len = dom1["inner_html_length"]
333	
334	            screenshot_path = str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "step1_scrolled_to_middle.png")
335	            await page.screenshot(path=screenshot_path, full_page=False)
336	            print(f"  Screenshot saved: {screenshot_path}")
337	
338	            # ==================================================================
339	            # Step 2: Collapse Citations
340	            # ==================================================================
341	            print("\n" + "=" * 70)
342	            print("STEP 2: Collapse Citations")
343	            print("=" * 70)
344	
345	            await page.click("#ws-section-citations .ws-section-header")
346	            await page.wait_for_timeout(COLLAPSE_SETTLE_MS)
347	
348	            dom2 = await page.evaluate(JS_INSPECT_DOM)
349	            _print_dom_state("step2", dom2)
350	
351	            check("step2", "section_collapsed", "collapsed" in dom2["section_classes"],
352	                  "True (collapsed)", "collapsed" in dom2["section_classes"])
353	            # Cards should still be in DOM (just hidden by CSS)
354	            check("step2", "card_count_in_dom", dom2["card_count"],
355	                  f"== {step1_card_count} (cards preserved in DOM)",
356	                  dom2["card_count"] == step1_card_count)
357	            check("step2", "html_preserved", dom2["inner_html_length"],
358	                  f"== {step1_html_len} (innerHTML unchanged)",
359	                  dom2["inner_html_length"] == step1_html_len)
360	
361	            step2_html_len = dom2["inner_html_length"]
362	            step2_card_count = dom2["card_count"]
363	
364	            screenshot_path = str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "step2_collapsed.png")
365	            await page.screenshot(path=screenshot_path, full_page=False)
366	            print(f"  Screenshot saved: {screenshot_path}")
367	
368	            # ==================================================================
369	            # Step 3: Scroll report while Citations is collapsed
370	            #   Guard in renderCitationSidebar should prevent any changes
371	            # ==================================================================
372	            print("\n" + "=" * 70)
373	            print("STEP 3: Scroll report while Citations is collapsed")
374	            print("=" * 70)
375	
376	            # Scroll to top
377	            await page.evaluate(
378	                "() => { document.getElementById('ws-thread').scrollTop = 0; }"
379	            )
380	            await page.wait_for_timeout(SCROLL_SETTLE_MS)
381	
382	            dom3a = await page.evaluate(JS_INSPECT_DOM)
383	            _print_dom_state("step3a (scrolled to top while collapsed)", dom3a)
384	
385	            check("step3a", "card_count_unchanged", dom3a["card_count"],
386	                  f"== {step2_card_count} (guard prevents overwrite)",
387	                  dom3a["card_count"] == step2_card_count)
388	            check("step3a", "html_unchanged", dom3a["inner_html_length"],
389	                  f"== {step2_html_len} (guard prevents overwrite)",
390	                  dom3a["inner_html_length"] == step2_html_len)
391	
392	            # Scroll to bottom
393	            await page.evaluate(
394	                "() => { document.getElementById('ws-thread').scrollTop = 999999; }"
395	            )
396	            await page.wait_for_timeout(SCROLL_SETTLE_MS)
397	
398	            dom3b = await page.evaluate(JS_INSPECT_DOM)
399	            _print_dom_state("step3b (scrolled to bottom while collapsed)", dom3b)
400	
401	            check("step3b", "card_count_unchanged", dom3b["card_count"],
402	                  f"== {step2_card_count} (guard prevents overwrite)",
403	                  dom3b["card_count"] == step2_card_count)
404	            check("step3b", "html_unchanged", dom3b["inner_html_length"],
405	                  f"== {step2_html_len} (guard prevents overwrite)",
406	                  dom3b["inner_html_length"] == step2_html_len)
407	
408	            screenshot_path = str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "step3_scrolled_while_collapsed.png")
409	            await page.screenshot(path=screenshot_path, full_page=False)
410	            print(f"  Screenshot saved: {screenshot_path}")
411

1	"""
2	Playwright bug reproduction: collapse/re-expand Citations shows #ws-source-feed
3	instead of #ws-citation-list.
4	
5	Bug mechanism: After a running->report phase transition, inline display styles on
6	#ws-source-feed and #ws-citation-list can become stale. When the Citations section
7	is collapsed and re-expanded, the source feed (running-phase content showing
8	"Sources will appear here as they are discovered...") appears instead of the
9	citation list (report-phase citation cards).
10	
11	The fix: _toggleRightSection("citations") now calls _updateCitationsSectionContent()
12	on expand, which resets inline styles based on the current _wsPhase.
13	
14	Usage:
15	    python scripts/playwright_cite_sourcefeed_bug.py
16	
17	Outputs screenshots to outputs/cite_sourcefeed_bug/
18	"""
19	
20	import asyncio
21	import json
22	import os
23	import socket
24	import subprocess
25	import sys
26	import time
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	# Configuration (LAW VI: no hard-coded values in logic)
31	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
32	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
33	SCREENSHOT_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "outputs" / "cite_sourcefeed_bug"
34	SERVER_SCRIPT = PROJECT_ROOT / "scripts" / "live_server.py"
35	SERVER_LOG = PROJECT_ROOT / "logs" / "cite_sourcefeed_bug_server.log"
36	PAGE_LOAD_WAIT_MS = 3000
37	SETUP_WAIT_MS = 2000
38	COLLAPSE_SETTLE_MS = 300
39	EXPAND_SETTLE_MS = 500
40	PHASE_TRANSITION_WAIT_MS = 500
41	
42	
43	def _find_free_port() -> int:
44	    """Bind to port 0 and let the OS assign a free port."""
45	    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
46	        s.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
47	        return s.getsockname()[1]
48	
49	
50	def _wait_for_server(port: int, timeout: float = 15.0) -> bool:
51	    """Poll until the server accepts TCP connections."""
52	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
53	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
54	        try:
55	            with socket.create_connection(("127.0.0.1", port), timeout=1.0):
56	                return True
57	        except OSError:
58	            time.sleep(0.3)
59	    return False

87	            "## Section 2\\n\\nMore content [4][5][6].\\n\\n" +

89	        appendReportBlock(reportMd, state.bibliography);

155	def _print_diagnosis(label: str, diag: dict) -> None:
156	    """Print a structured diagnosis block."""
157	    print(f"\n  --- {label} ---")

163	    print(f"  #ws-citation-list inline:   '{diag['cite_list_inline_display']}'")

165	    print(f"  #ws-citation-list computed: '{diag['cite_list_computed_display']}'")
166	    print(f"  #ws-source-feed HTML[:100]: {diag['src_feed_inner_100'][:80]}")
167	    print(f"  #ws-citation-list HTML[:100]: {diag['cite_list_inner_100'][:80]}")

170	def _check(
171	    results: dict,
172	    step: str,
173	    key: str,
174	    actual,
175	    expected,
176	    assertion_fn,
177	) -> bool:
178	    """Record a PASS/FAIL check and return the boolean."""
179	    passed = assertion_fn(actual)
180	    status = "PASS" if passed else "FAIL"
181	    results.setdefault(step, [])
182	    results[step].append({
183	        "key": key,
184	        "actual": actual,
185	        "expected": expected,
186	        "status": status,
187	    })
188	    print(f"  [{status}] {step}.{key} = {repr(actual)} (expected: {expected})")
189	    return passed

366	            await page.wait_for_timeout(EXPAND_SETTLE_MS)
367	
368	            diag3 = await page.evaluate(JS_DIAGNOSE_STATE)
369	            _print_diagnosis("Step 3 Diagnosis (re-expanded, FIXED)", diag3)
370	
371	            # The fix: _toggleRightSection calls _updateCitationsSectionContent
372	            # on expand, which resets inline styles based on _wsPhase (report).
373	            # This is THE critical assertion proving the fix works.
374	            p = _check(results, "step3", "src_feed_computed_none_FIXED", diag3["src_feed_computed_display"], "none (fixed!)", lambda v: v == "none")
375	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
376	
377	            p = _check(results, "step3", "cite_list_computed_visible_FIXED", diag3["cite_list_computed_display"], "NOT none (fixed!)", lambda v: v != "none")
378	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
379	
380	            # Cite cards survive collapse/re-expand (scroll_sync may limit count)
381	            p = _check(
382	                results, "step3", "cite_card_count",
383	                diag3["cite_card_count"],
384	                ">= 1 (cards survive collapse/re-expand)",
385	                lambda v: v >= 1,
386	            )
387	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
388	
389	            p = _check(results, "step3", "section_expanded", diag3["section_class"], "expanded", lambda v: v == "expanded")
390	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
391	
392	            # Verify cite_list inner HTML has card content, NOT source feed text
393	            p = _check(
394	                results, "step3", "cite_list_has_cards_not_placeholder",
395	                "ws-cite-card" in diag3["cite_list_inner_100"],
396	                "True (cite-card elements present in #ws-citation-list)",
397	                lambda v: v is True,
398	            )
399	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
400	
401	            ss_path = str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "step3_reexpanded_fixed.png")
402	            await page.screenshot(path=ss_path, full_page=False)
403	            screenshots.append(ss_path)
404	            print(f"  Screenshot saved: {ss_path}")
405	
406	            # ================================================================
407	            # STEP 4: Natural flow (no manual corruption)
408	            # ================================================================
409	            print("\n" + "=" * 60)
410	            print("STEP 4: Natural flow -- running->report, collapse, re-expand")
411	            print("=" * 60)
412	
413	            # Reset to running
414	            reset_result = await page.evaluate(JS_RESET_RUNNING)
415	            print(f"  Reset result: {reset_result}")
416	            await page.wait_for_timeout(PHASE_TRANSITION_WAIT_MS)
417	
418	            # Transition to report with citations (direct render, no appendReportBlock)
419	            nat_result = await page.evaluate(JS_NATURAL_REPORT_TRANSITION)
420	            print(f"  Natural report transition result: {nat_result}")
421	            await page.wait_for_timeout(PHASE_TRANSITION_WAIT_MS)
422	
423	            diag4a = await page.evaluate(JS_DIAGNOSE_STATE)
424	            _print_diagnosis("Step 4a: After natural transition", diag4a)
425	
426	            # Collapse Citations
427	            print("  Collapsing Citations...")
428	            await page.click("#ws-section-citations .ws-section-header")
429	            await page.wait_for_timeout(COLLAPSE_SETTLE_MS)
430	
431	            diag4b = await page.evaluate(JS_DIAGNOSE_STATE)
432	            _print_diagnosis("Step 4b: After collapse", diag4b)
433	
434	            p = _check(results, "step4", "collapsed_class", diag4b["section_class"], "collapsed", lambda v: v == "collapsed")
435	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
436	
437	            # Re-expand Citations
438	            print("  Re-expanding Citations...")
439	            await page.click("#ws-section-citations .ws-section-header")
440	            await page.wait_for_timeout(EXPAND_SETTLE_MS)
441	
442	            diag4c = await page.evaluate(JS_DIAGNOSE_STATE)
443	            _print_diagnosis("Step 4c: After re-expand", diag4c)
444	
445	            p = _check(results, "step4", "src_feed_display_after_reexpand", diag4c["src_feed_computed_display"], "none", lambda v: v == "none")
446	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
447	
448	            p = _check(results, "step4", "cite_list_display_after_reexpand", diag4c["cite_list_computed_display"], "NOT none", lambda v: v != "none")
449	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
450	
451	            # Step 4 uses direct renderCitationSidebar (no scroll_sync), so all 20 cards
452	            p = _check(results, "step4", "cite_card_count_after_reexpand", diag4c["cite_card_count"], "20", lambda v: v == 20)
453	            total_pass += int(p); total_fail += int(not p)
454	
455	            ss_path = str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "step4_natural_flow.png")
456	            await page.screenshot(path=ss_path, full_page=False)
457	            screenshots.append(ss_path)
458	            print(f"  Screenshot saved: {ss_path}")
459	
460	            # ================================================================
461	            # Console errors (filter out favicon 404s which are expected)
462	            # ================================================================
463	            real_errors = [e for e in console_errors if "favicon" not in e.lower() and "404" not in e]
464	            if real_errors:
465	                print(f"\n[TEST] Non-favicon console errors ({len(real_errors)}):")
466	                for err in real_errors[:10]:
467	                    print(f"  {err}")
468	            elif console_errors:
469	                print(f"\n[TEST] {len(console_errors)} console errors (all favicon 404s, expected)")
470	
471	            await browser.close()
472	
473	    finally:
474	        # Terminate server
475	        server_proc.terminate()
476	        try:
477	            server_proc.wait(timeout=5)
478	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
479	            server_proc.kill()
480	        log_fh.close()
481	        print(f"\n[TEST] Server terminated. Log at: {SERVER_LOG}")
482	
483	    # ================================================================
484	    # Summary
485	    # ================================================================
486	    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
487	    print("TEST SUMMARY")
488	    print("=" * 60)
489	    print(f"  Total PASS: {total_pass}")
490	    print(f"  Total FAIL: {total_fail}")
491	    print(f"  Overall:    {'ALL PASS' if total_fail == 0 else 'FAILURES DETECTED'}")
492	    print(f"\n  Screenshots ({len(screenshots)}):")
493	    for ss in screenshots:
494	        print(f"    {ss}")
495	
496	    # Write results JSON
497	    summary_path = str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "test_results.json")
498	    with open(summary_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
499	        json.dump(
500	            {
501	                "total_pass": total_pass,
502	                "total_fail": total_fail,
503	                "overall": "PASS" if total_fail == 0 else "FAIL",
504	                "screenshots": screenshots,
505	                "results": results,
506	            },
507	            f,
508	            indent=2,
509	        )
510	    print(f"  Results JSON: {summary_path}")
511	
512	    return total_fail == 0
513	
514	
515	if __name__ == "__main__":
516	    success = asyncio.run(run_test())
517	    sys.exit(0 if success else 1)
518

283	class ServerLifecycle:
284	    """Start/stop live_server.py (mirrors visual_qa_audit.py:475 pattern)."""
285	
286	    def __init__(self, port: int):
287	        self.port = port
288	        self.process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
289	        self.log_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "server.log"
290	
291	    async def start(self) -> None:
292	        """Start the live server and wait for it to become healthy."""
293	        OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
294	        log.info("Starting live_server.py on port %d ...", self.port)
295	        log_file = open(self.log_path, "w")
296	        self.process = subprocess.Popen(
297	            [
298	                sys.executable, "-u",
299	                str(_PROJECT_ROOT / "scripts" / "live_server.py"),
300	                "--port", str(self.port),
301	            ],
302	            stdout=log_file,
303	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
304	            cwd=str(_PROJECT_ROOT),
305	        )
306	        import aiohttp
307	        url = f"http://localhost:{self.port}/health"
308	        for attempt in range(30):
309	            await asyncio.sleep(1)
310	            try:
311	                async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
312	                    async with session.get(url, timeout=aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=3)) as resp:
313	                        if resp.status == 200:
314	                            log.info("Server healthy after %d seconds", attempt + 1)
315	                            return
316	            except Exception:
317	                pass
318	            if self.process.poll() is not None:
319	                raise RuntimeError(
320	                    f"Server process exited with code {self.process.returncode}. "
321	                    f"Check {self.log_path}"
322	                )
323	        raise RuntimeError(f"Server did not become healthy within 30s. Check {self.log_path}")
324	
325	    async def stop(self) -> None:
326	        """Stop the server process."""
327	        if self.process and self.process.poll() is None:
328	            log.info("Stopping server (PID %d) ...", self.process.pid)
329	            self.process.terminate()
330	            try:
331	                self.process.wait(timeout=5)
332	            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
333	                self.process.kill()
334	                self.process.wait(timeout=3)
335	            log.info("Server stopped.")
336

385	
386	    def __init__(self, port: int):
387	        self.port = port
388	        self.base_url = f"http://localhost:{port}"
389	
390	    async def setup_page(self, page: Page) -> None:
391	        """Install mock routes and freeze dynamic content on a fresh page."""
392	        # Freeze animations and timers
393	        await page.emulate_media(reduced_motion="reduce")
394	        await page.add_init_script("""
395	            const _origSetInterval = window.setInterval;
396	            window.setInterval = function(fn, ms, ...args) {
397	                if (ms >= 500) return _origSetInterval.call(window, () => {}, ms);
398	                return _origSetInterval.call(window, fn, ms, ...args);
399	            };
400	            const _frozenNow = 1709568000000;
401	            Date.now = () => _frozenNow;
402	            Math.random = () => 0.42;
403	            window._designAuditMode = true;
404	        """)
405	        # Mock API routes — Playwright calls handler(route, request),
406	        # so we must absorb the request arg to avoid overwriting the closure.
407	        for pattern, mock in self.MOCK_ROUTES.items():
408	            async def _make_handler(m):
409	                async def _handler(route):
410	                    await route.fulfill(
411	                        status=m["status"],
412	                        content_type=m["content_type"],
413	                        body=m["body"],
414	                    )
415	                return _handler
416	            await page.route(pattern, await _make_handler(mock))
417	
418	    async def navigate_to_state(self, page: Page, state: dict[str, Any], theme: str) -> None:
419	        """Navigate the page to a specific state and theme."""
420	        # Navigate to base URL — use domcontentloaded (not networkidle)
421	        # because the dashboard keeps SSE/WebSocket connections open.
422	        await page.goto(self.base_url, wait_until="domcontentloaded", timeout=15000)
423	        await page.wait_for_timeout(500)  # let JS initialize
424	
425	        # Set theme
426	        await page.evaluate(f"document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', '{theme}')")
427	        await page.wait_for_timeout(100)
428	
429	        # Execute state setup
430	        setup_js = state["setup"]
431	        try:
432	            await page.evaluate(setup_js)
433	        except Exception as exc:
434	            log.warning("State setup failed for %s: %s", state["id"], exc)
435	
436	        # Post-wait for states that need DOM to settle
437	        post_wait = state.get("post_wait", 300)
438	        await page.wait_for_timeout(post_wait)
439	
440	        # Handle post-navigation actions
441	        if state.get("post_navigate") == "citation_modal":
442	            await self._open_citation_modal(page)
443	
444	    async def _open_citation_modal(self, page: Page) -> None:
445	        """Open a citation chain modal if available."""
446	        try:
447	            await page.evaluate("""
448	                const citeLink = document.querySelector('.cite-link, .cite-ref');
449	                if (citeLink) citeLink.click();
450	            """)
451	            await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
452	        except Exception:
453	            log.warning("Could not open citation modal")
454

460	class ScreenshotCapture:
461	    """Capture and name screenshots consistently."""
462	
463	    def __init__(self, output_dir: Path):
464	        self.output_dir = output_dir
465	
466	    def screenshot_path(self, state_name: str, theme: str, viewport: str, phase: str) -> Path:
467	        """Generate consistent screenshot file path."""
468	        folder = self.output_dir / phase
469	        folder.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
470	        return folder / f"{state_name}_{theme}_{viewport}.png"
471	
472	    async def capture(self, page: Page, state_name: str, theme: str,
473	                      viewport: str, phase: str) -> Path:
474	        """Take a full-page screenshot and save it."""
475	        path = self.screenshot_path(state_name, theme, viewport, phase)
476	        await page.screenshot(path=str(path), full_page=False)
477	        log.info("  Screenshot: %s (%d bytes)", path.name, path.stat().st_size)
478	        return path

481	# =========================================================================
482	# HEURISTIC ENGINE — 11 automated DOM measurements
483	# =========================================================================
484	
485	class HeuristicEngine:
486	    """Run H1-H11 DOM measurement checks via page.evaluate()."""
487	
488	    async def run_all(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> list[HeuristicResult]:
489	        """Run all heuristic checks and return results."""
490	        results = []
491	        checks = [
492	            self.h1_touch_targets,
493	            self.h2_radius_system,
494	            self.h3_spacing_rhythm,
495	            self.h4_column_alignment,
496	            self.h5_typography_hierarchy,
497	            self.h6_surface_hierarchy,
498	            self.h7_state_clarity,
499	            self.h8_workflow_clarity,
500	            self.h9_content_density,
501	            self.h10_scroll_behavior,
502	            self.h11_visual_balance,
503	        ]
504	        for check in checks:
505	            try:
506	                result = await check(page, viewport_name)
507	                results.append(result)
508	                status = "PASS" if result.passed else "FAIL"
509	                log.info("  %s %s: %.0f%% (threshold %.0f%%)",
510	                         status, result.id, result.score * 100, result.threshold * 100)
511	            except Exception as exc:
512	                log.error("  ERROR %s: %s", check.__name__, exc)
513	                results.append(HeuristicResult(
514	                    id=check.__name__.split("_")[0].upper(),
515	                    name=check.__name__,
516	                    passed=False,
517	                    score=0.0,
518	                    threshold=0.0,
519	                    violations=[{"error": str(exc)}],
520	                ))
521	        return results
522	
523	    # ----- H1: Touch Target Compliance -----
524	    async def h1_touch_targets(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
525	        is_mobile = "mobile" in viewport_name or "375" in viewport_name
526	        min_size = 44 if is_mobile else 36
527	        threshold = 1.0 if is_mobile else 0.95
528	
529	        data = await page.evaluate(f"""(() => {{
530	            const selectors = 'button, input:not([type="hidden"]), [role="tab"], [role="switch"], ' +
531	                '.phase-row, .ev-card, .filter-chip, .seg-btn, .adv-tab-btn, ' +
532	                '.depth-chip, .chain-tab, .nav-btn, .view-mode-btn, .density-btn, ' +
533	                '.export-btn, .bookmark-btn, .detail-panel-close, .pipe-tool-btn, ' +
534	                '.pipeline-new-btn, .pipeline-tpl-btn, .wizard-chip, .toc-link, ' +
535	                '.mem-item-delete, .user-progress-cancel, .mem-refresh-btn, ' +
536	                '.mem-search-input, .chain-modal-close, .export-btn-audit, .auth-field';
537	            const isDense = document.body.classList.contains('operator-dense');
538	            const els = document.querySelectorAll(selectors);
539	            const minSize = isDense ? Math.min({min_size}, 24) : {min_size};
540	            let total = 0, passing = 0;
541	            const violations = [];
542	            for (const el of els) {{
543	                if (el.offsetParent === null) continue;
544	                const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
545	                if (rect.width === 0 && rect.height === 0) continue;
546	                // Skip elements inside collapsed/hidden containers (< 12px in either dimension)
547	                if (rect.width < 12 || rect.height < 12) continue;
548	                // Skip checkbox/radio/color inputs (browser-styled, not actionable targets)
549	                const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase();
550	                if (tag === 'input' && ['checkbox', 'radio', 'color', 'range', 'hidden'].includes(el.type)) continue;
551	                total++;
552	                const ok = rect.width >= minSize && rect.height >= minSize;
553	                if (ok) passing++;
554	                else violations.push({{
555	                    selector: tag + (el.className ? '.' + el.className.split(' ')[0] : ''),
556	                    width: Math.round(rect.width),
557	                    height: Math.round(rect.height),
558	                    minRequired: minSize,
559	                }});
560	            }}
561	            return {{ total, passing, violations: violations.slice(0, 20), isDense }};
562	        }})()""")
563	
564	        score = data["passing"] / max(data["total"], 1)
565	        return HeuristicResult(
566	            id="H1", name="Touch Target Compliance",
567	            passed=score >= threshold, score=score, threshold=threshold,
568	            violations=data["violations"],
569	            evidence={"total": data["total"], "passing": data["passing"], "min_size": min_size},
570	        )
571	
572	    # ----- H2: Radius System Consistency -----
573	    async def h2_radius_system(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
574	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
575	            const validTokens = new Set([
576	                '0px', '3px', '4px', '5px', '6px', '8px', '12px', '20px', '9999px', '50%'
577	            ]);
578	            const all = document.querySelectorAll('*');
579	            let total = 0, onToken = 0;
580	            const violations = [];
581	            for (const el of all) {
582	                if (el.offsetParent === null && el !== document.body) continue;
583	                const r = getComputedStyle(el).borderRadius;
584	                if (r === '0px' || r === '') continue;
585	                total++;
586	                const corners = r.split(' ').map(v => v.trim());
587	                const allValid = corners.every(c => validTokens.has(c));
588	                if (allValid) { onToken++; }
589	                else if (violations.length < 15) {
590	                    violations.push({
591	                        selector: el.tagName.toLowerCase() + (el.className ? '.' + el.className.split(' ')[0] : ''),
592	                        value: r,
593	                    });
594	                }
595	            }
596	            return { total, onToken, violations };
597	        })()""")
598	
599	        score = data["onToken"] / max(data["total"], 1)
600	        return HeuristicResult(
601	            id="H2", name="Radius System Consistency",
602	            passed=score >= 0.90, score=score, threshold=0.90,
603	            violations=data["violations"],
604	            evidence={"total": data["total"], "on_token": data["onToken"]},
605	        )
606	
607	    # ----- H3: Spacing Rhythm (4px Grid) -----
608	    async def h3_spacing_rhythm(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
609	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
610	            const containers = document.querySelectorAll(
611	                '.metric-grid, .metric-card, .card, .phase-list-column, .phase-detail-column, ' +
612	                '.pipeline-column, .ev-card, .report-body, .report-rendered, .adv-pane, ' +
613	                '.filter-chips, .gate-grid, .source-card, .evidence-cards-area'
614	            );
615	            let total = 0, onGrid = 0;
616	            const violations = [];
617	            const exempt = new Set([1, 2, 3]);
618	            for (const el of containers) {
619	                if (el.offsetParent === null && el !== document.body) continue;
620	                const cs = getComputedStyle(el);
621	                const props = ['paddingTop', 'paddingRight', 'paddingBottom', 'paddingLeft',
622	                               'marginTop', 'marginRight', 'marginBottom', 'marginLeft', 'gap'];
623	                for (const prop of props) {
624	                    const raw = cs[prop];
625	                    if (!raw || raw === 'normal') continue;
626	                    const val = parseFloat(raw);
627	                    if (isNaN(val) || val === 0) continue;
628	                    total++;
629	                    if (exempt.has(val) || val % 4 === 0) { onGrid++; }
630	                    else if (violations.length < 15) {
631	                        violations.push({
632	                            selector: el.tagName.toLowerCase() + (el.className ? '.' + el.className.split(' ')[0] : ''),
633	                            property: prop,
634	                            value: raw,
635	                        });
636	                    }
637	                }
638	            }
639	            return { total, onGrid, violations };
640	        })()""")
641	
642	        score = data["onGrid"] / max(data["total"], 1)
643	        return HeuristicResult(
644	            id="H3", name="Spacing Rhythm (4px Grid)",
645	            passed=score >= 0.85 or data["total"] == 0,
646	            score=score, threshold=0.85,
647	            violations=data["violations"],
648	            evidence={"total": data["total"], "on_grid": data["onGrid"]},
649	        )
650	
651	    # ----- H4: Column Alignment -----
652	    async def h4_column_alignment(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
653	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
654	            const columns = document.querySelectorAll(
655	                '.phase-list-column, .phase-detail-column, .pipeline-column, ' +
656	                '.evidence-cards-area, .adv-pane'
657	            );
658	            let total = 0, aligned = 0;
659	            const violations = [];
660	            const tolerance = 4;
661	            for (const col of columns) {
662	                if (col.offsetParent === null) continue;
663	                const children = [...col.children].filter(c => {
664	                    if (c.offsetParent === null) return false;
665	                    const r = c.getBoundingClientRect();
666	                    // Exclude off-screen/collapsed elements
667	                    return r.width > 0 && r.height > 0 && r.left >= -10;
668	                });
669	                if (children.length < 2) continue;
670	                const leftPositions = children.map(c => Math.round(c.getBoundingClientRect().left));
671	                const mode = leftPositions.sort((a,b) => a - b)[Math.floor(leftPositions.length / 2)];
672	                for (let i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {
673	                    total++;
674	                    const left = Math.round(children[i].getBoundingClientRect().left);
675	                    if (Math.abs(left - mode) <= tolerance) { aligned++; }
676	                    else if (violations.length < 10) {
677	                        violations.push({
678	                            column: col.className.split(' ')[0],
679	                            child_index: i,
680	                            expected_left: mode,
681	                            actual_left: left,
682	                            delta: left - mode,
683	                        });
684	                    }
685	                }
686	            }
687	            return { total, aligned, violations };
688	        })()""")
689	
690	        score = data["aligned"] / max(data["total"], 1)
691	        return HeuristicResult(
692	            id="H4", name="Column Alignment",
693	            passed=score >= 0.95 or data["total"] == 0,
694	            score=score, threshold=0.95,
695	            violations=data["violations"],
696	            evidence={"total": data["total"], "aligned": data["aligned"]},
697	        )
698	
699	    # ----- H5: Typography Hierarchy -----
700	    async def h5_typography_hierarchy(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
701	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
702	            const hierarchy = [
703	                { selector: 'h1, .report-body h1, .report-rendered h1', role: 'h1' },
704	                { selector: 'h2, .report-body h2, .report-rendered h2', role: 'h2' },
705	                { selector: 'h3, .report-body h3, .report-rendered h3', role: 'h3' },
706	                { selector: '.section-title', role: 'section-title' },
707	                { selector: '.report-body p, .report-rendered p, body', role: 'body' },
708	                { selector: '.label, .metric-card .label', role: 'label' },
709	                { selector: '.tier-badge, .nav-badge', role: 'badge' },
710	            ];
711	            const sizes = {};
712	            for (const level of hierarchy) {
713	                const el = document.querySelector(level.selector);
714	                if (el && el.offsetParent !== null) {
715	                    sizes[level.role] = parseFloat(getComputedStyle(el).fontSize);
716	                }
717	            }
718	            const violations = [];
719	            const order = ['h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'section-title', 'body', 'label', 'badge'];
720	            let inversions = 0;
721	            let comparisons = 0;
722	            for (let i = 0; i < order.length - 1; i++) {
723	                const a = order[i], b = order[i + 1];
724	                if (sizes[a] !== undefined && sizes[b] !== undefined) {
725	                    comparisons++;
726	                    if (sizes[a] < sizes[b]) {
727	                        inversions++;
728	                        violations.push({
729	                            higher: a, higher_size: sizes[a],
730	                            lower: b, lower_size: sizes[b],
731	                        });
732	                    }
733	                }
734	            }
735	            return { sizes, violations, comparisons, inversions };
736	        })()""")
737	
738	        score = 1.0 - (data["inversions"] / max(data["comparisons"], 1))
739	        return HeuristicResult(
740	            id="H5", name="Typography Hierarchy",
741	            passed=data["inversions"] == 0, score=score, threshold=1.0,
742	            violations=data["violations"],
743	            evidence={"sizes": data["sizes"], "comparisons": data["comparisons"]},
744	        )
745	
746	    # ----- H6: Surface Hierarchy (Background Depth) -----
747	    async def h6_surface_hierarchy(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
748	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
749	            function getLuminance(rgb) {
750	                const match = rgb.match(/\\d+/g);
751	                if (!match || match.length < 3) return 0;
752	                const [r, g, b] = match.map(Number);
753	                return 0.299 * r + 0.587 * g + 0.114 * b;
754	            }
755	            const isDark = document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') !== 'light';
756	            const surfaces = [
757	                { selector: 'body', role: 'bg-primary' },
758	                { selector: '.phase-list-column, .pipeline-column', role: 'bg-secondary' },
759	                { selector: '.metric-card, .ev-card, .report-body', role: 'bg-card' },
760	                { selector: '.phase-block.expanded, .toast, .cite-popover', role: 'bg-elevated' },
761	            ];
762	            const lums = {};
763	            for (const s of surfaces) {
764	                const el = document.querySelector(s.selector);
765	                if (el) {
766	                    const bg = getComputedStyle(el).backgroundColor;
767	                    lums[s.role] = getLuminance(bg);
768	                }
769	            }
770	            const order = ['bg-primary', 'bg-secondary', 'bg-card', 'bg-elevated'];
771	            let violations = [];
772	            let comparisons = 0;
773	            let correct = 0;
774	            const TOLERANCE = 5;  // Allow <=5 luminance delta in light themes (shadow-based depth)
775	            for (let i = 0; i < order.length - 1; i++) {
776	                const a = order[i], b = order[i + 1];
777	                if (lums[a] !== undefined && lums[b] !== undefined) {
778	                    comparisons++;
779	                    const delta = Math.abs(lums[a] - lums[b]);
780	                    if (isDark) {
781	                        if (lums[a] <= lums[b]) correct++;
782	                        else violations.push({ a, lum_a: lums[a], b, lum_b: lums[b], expected: 'a <= b (dark)' });
783	                    } else {
784	                        if (lums[a] >= lums[b] || delta <= TOLERANCE) correct++;
785	                        else violations.push({ a, lum_a: lums[a], b, lum_b: lums[b], delta, expected: 'a >= b (light, tolerance 5)' });
786	                    }
787	                }
788	            }
789	            return { lums, violations, comparisons, correct };
790	        })()""")
791	
792	        score = data["correct"] / max(data["comparisons"], 1)
793	        return HeuristicResult(
794	            id="H6", name="Surface Hierarchy (Background Depth)",
795	            passed=len(data["violations"]) == 0, score=score, threshold=1.0,
796	            violations=data["violations"],
797	            evidence={"luminances": data["lums"], "comparisons": data["comparisons"]},
798	        )
799	
800	    # ----- H7: State Clarity -----
801	    async def h7_state_clarity(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
802	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
803	            function getProps(el) {
804	                const cs = getComputedStyle(el);
805	                return {
806	                    color: cs.color,
807	                    backgroundColor: cs.backgroundColor,
808	                    borderColor: cs.borderColor || cs.borderBottomColor,
809	                };
810	            }
811	            function luminance(rgb) {
812	                const m = rgb.match(/[\\d.]+/g);
813	                if (!m || m.length < 3) return 0;
814	                return 0.299 * +m[0] + 0.587 * +m[1] + 0.114 * +m[2];
815	            }
816	            function colorDistance(rgb1, rgb2) {
817	                const m1 = rgb1.match(/[\\d.]+/g);
818	                const m2 = rgb2.match(/[\\d.]+/g);
819	                if (!m1 || m1.length < 3 || !m2 || m2.length < 3) return 0;
820	                const dr = +m1[0] - +m2[0], dg = +m1[1] - +m2[1], db = +m1[2] - +m2[2];
821	                return Math.sqrt(dr*dr + dg*dg + db*db);
822	            }
823	            const types = [
824	                { selector: '.nav-btn', activeClass: 'active' },
825	                { selector: '.phase-row', activeClass: 'selected' },
826	                { selector: '.filter-chip', activeClass: 'active' },
827	                { selector: '.seg-btn', activeClass: 'active' },
828	                { selector: '.adv-tab-btn', activeClass: 'active' },
829	                { selector: '.depth-chip', activeClass: 'active' },
830	            ];
831	            let total = 0, clear = 0;
832	            const violations = [];
833	            for (const t of types) {
834	                const els = document.querySelectorAll(t.selector);
835	                const defaultEl = [...els].find(e => !e.classList.contains(t.activeClass) && e.offsetParent !== null);
836	                const activeEl = [...els].find(e => e.classList.contains(t.activeClass) && e.offsetParent !== null);
837	                if (!defaultEl || !activeEl) continue;
838	                total++;
839	                const defProps = getProps(defaultEl);
840	                const actProps = getProps(activeEl);
841	                let changes = 0;
842	                if (defProps.color !== actProps.color) changes++;
843	                if (defProps.backgroundColor !== actProps.backgroundColor) changes++;
844	                if (defProps.borderColor !== actProps.borderColor) changes++;
845	                const lumDelta = Math.abs(luminance(defProps.color) - luminance(actProps.color));
846	                const colDist = colorDistance(defProps.color, actProps.color);
847	                const bgDist = colorDistance(defProps.backgroundColor, actProps.backgroundColor);
848	                if (changes >= 2 && (lumDelta > 30 || colDist > 80 || bgDist > 40)) { clear++; }
849	                else {
850	                    violations.push({
851	                        selector: t.selector,
852	                        changes,
853	                        lum_delta: Math.round(lumDelta),
854	                        color_distance: Math.round(colDist),
855	                        default_props: defProps,
856	                        active_props: actProps,
857	                    });
858	                }
859	            }
860	            return { total, clear, violations };
861	        })()""")
862	
863	        score = data["clear"] / max(data["total"], 1)
864	        return HeuristicResult(
865	            id="H7", name="State Clarity",
866	            passed=score >= 1.0 or data["total"] == 0,
867	            score=score, threshold=1.0,
868	            violations=data["violations"],
869	            evidence={"total": data["total"], "clear": data["clear"]},
870	        )
871	
872	    # ----- H8: Workflow Clarity -----
873	    async def h8_workflow_clarity(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
874	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
875	            const navBtns = document.querySelectorAll('.nav-btn[data-view]');
876	            let total = 0, correct = 0;
877	            const violations = [];
878	            for (const btn of navBtns) {
879	                if (btn.offsetParent === null) continue;
880	                const viewName = btn.getAttribute('data-view');
881	                btn.click();
882	                total++;
883	                const activePane = document.querySelector('.view-pane.active');
884	                const activeBtn = document.querySelector('.nav-btn.active');
885	                const paneVisible = activePane && activePane.offsetHeight > 0;
886	                const paneMatches = activePane && activePane.id === 'view-' + viewName;
887	                const onlyOneActive = document.querySelectorAll('.nav-btn.active').length === 1;
888	                if (paneVisible && paneMatches && onlyOneActive) { correct++; }
889	                else {
890	                    violations.push({
891	                        button: viewName,
892	                        pane_visible: !!paneVisible,
893	                        pane_matches: !!paneMatches,
894	                        active_btn_count: document.querySelectorAll('.nav-btn.active').length,
895	                    });
896	                }
897	            }
898	            return { total, correct, violations };
899	        })()""")
900	
901	        score = data["correct"] / max(data["total"], 1)
902	        return HeuristicResult(
903	            id="H8", name="Workflow Clarity",
904	            passed=score >= 1.0 or data["total"] == 0,
905	            score=score, threshold=1.0,
906	            violations=data["violations"],
907	            evidence={"total": data["total"], "correct": data["correct"]},
908	        )
909	
910	    # ----- H9: Content Density -----
911	    async def h9_content_density(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
912	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
913	            const vw = window.innerWidth;
914	            const panels = [
915	                { selector: '.phase-list-column', name: 'phase-list', expect_min: 0.08, expect_max: 0.22 },
916	                { selector: '.phase-detail-column', name: 'detail', expect_min: 0.30, expect_max: 0.65 },
917	                { selector: '.pipeline-column', name: 'metrics-rail', expect_min: 0.15, expect_max: 0.35 },
918	            ];
919	            let total = 0, inRange = 0;
920	            const violations = [];
921	            for (const p of panels) {
922	                const el = document.querySelector(p.selector);
923	                if (!el || el.offsetParent === null) continue;
924	                const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
925	                if (rect.width === 0) continue;
926	                total++;
927	                const ratio = rect.width / vw;
928	                if (ratio >= p.expect_min && ratio <= p.expect_max) { inRange++; }
929	                else {
930	                    violations.push({
931	                        panel: p.name,
932	                        width_px: Math.round(rect.width),
933	                        ratio: Math.round(ratio * 100) / 100,
934	                        expected: [p.expect_min, p.expect_max],
935	                    });
936	                }
937	            }
938	            return { total, inRange, violations, viewport_width: vw };
939	        })()""")
940	
941	        score = data["inRange"] / max(data["total"], 1)
942	        return HeuristicResult(
943	            id="H9", name="Content Density",
944	            passed=score >= 1.0 or data["total"] == 0,
945	            score=score, threshold=1.0,
946	            violations=data["violations"],
947	            evidence={"total": data["total"], "in_range": data["inRange"],
948	                      "viewport_width": data["viewport_width"]},
949	        )
950	
951	    # ----- H10: Scroll Behavior -----
952	    async def h10_scroll_behavior(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
953	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
954	            const scrollables = document.querySelectorAll(
955	                '.phase-list, .phase-detail-stream, .pipeline-column, .activity-log, ' +
956	                '.evidence-cards-area, .report-view, .adv-pane'
957	            );
958	            let total = 0, correct = 0;
959	            const violations = [];
960	            for (const el of scrollables) {
961	                if (el.offsetParent === null) continue;
962	                const hasOverflow = el.scrollHeight > el.clientHeight + 2;
963	                if (!hasOverflow) continue;
964	                total++;
965	                const cs = getComputedStyle(el);
966	                const hasScroll = cs.overflowY === 'auto' || cs.overflowY === 'scroll';
967	                const hasGutter = cs.scrollbarGutter === 'stable' || cs.scrollbarGutter === 'stable both-edges';
968	                if (hasScroll || hasGutter) { correct++; }
969	                else {
970	                    violations.push({
971	                        selector: el.className.split(' ')[0],
972	                        scrollHeight: el.scrollHeight,
973	                        clientHeight: el.clientHeight,
974	                        overflowY: cs.overflowY,
975	                        scrollbarGutter: cs.scrollbarGutter,
976	                    });
977	                }
978	            }
979	            return { total, correct, violations };
980	        })()""")
981	
982	        score = data["correct"] / max(data["total"], 1)
983	        return HeuristicResult(
984	            id="H10", name="Scroll Behavior",
985	            passed=score >= 1.0 or data["total"] == 0,
986	            score=score, threshold=1.0,
987	            violations=data["violations"],
988	            evidence={"total": data["total"], "correct": data["correct"]},
989	        )
990	
991	    # ----- H11: Visual Balance (Panel Proportions) -----
992	    async def h11_visual_balance(self, page: Page, viewport_name: str) -> HeuristicResult:
993	        data = await page.evaluate("""(() => {
994	            const tolerance = 5;
995	            const checks = [];
996	
997	            // Research view: 200px | 1fr | 340px
998	            const rv = document.querySelector('.research-view');
999	            if (rv && rv.offsetParent !== null) {
1000	                const cols = getComputedStyle(rv).gridTemplateColumns;
1001	                if (cols && cols !== 'none') {
1002	                    const widths = cols.split(' ').map(v => parseFloat(v));
1003	                    if (widths.length >= 3) {
1004	                        checks.push({
1005	                            name: 'research-phase-list',
1006	                            expected: 200,
1007	                            actual: Math.round(widths[0]),
1008	                            ok: Math.abs(widths[0] - 200) <= tolerance || widths[0] <= 210,
1009	                        });
1010	                        checks.push({
1011	                            name: 'research-metrics-rail',
1012	                            expected: 340,
1013	                            actual: Math.round(widths[2]),
1014	                            ok: Math.abs(widths[2] - 340) <= tolerance || (widths[2] >= 280 && widths[2] <= 400),
1015	                        });
1016	                    }
1017	                }
1018	            }
1019	            let total = checks.length;
1020	            let passing = checks.filter(c => c.ok).length;
1021	            const violations = checks.filter(c => !c.ok);
1022	            return { total, passing, checks, violations };
1023	        })()""")
1024	
1025	        score = data["passing"] / max(data["total"], 1)
1026	        return HeuristicResult(
1027	            id="H11", name="Visual Balance (Panel Proportions)",
1028	            passed=score >= 1.0 or data["total"] == 0,
1029	            score=score, threshold=1.0,
1030	            violations=data["violations"],
1031	            evidence={"checks": data["checks"]},
1032	        )

1035	# =========================================================================
1036	# COMPARISON ENGINE — pixel diff before/after
1037	# =========================================================================
1038	
1039	class ComparisonEngine:
1040	    """Generate pixel-diff images comparing before and after screenshots."""
1041	
1042	    def __init__(self, output_dir: Path):
1043	        self.output_dir = output_dir
1044	        self.comparisons_dir = output_dir / "comparisons"
1045	        self.comparisons_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1046	
1047	    def compare(self, before_path: Path, after_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
1048	        """Compare two screenshots and produce a diff image."""
1049	        if not before_path.exists() or not after_path.exists():
1050	            return {"error": "missing file", "before": str(before_path), "after": str(after_path)}
1051	
1052	        img_before = Image.open(before_path).convert("RGB")
1053	        img_after = Image.open(after_path).convert("RGB")
1054	
1055	        # Resize to match if needed
1056	        if img_before.size != img_after.size:
1057	            img_after = img_after.resize(img_before.size, Image.LANCZOS)
1058	
1059	        diff = ImageChops.difference(img_before, img_after)
1060	        diff_name = f"diff_{before_path.stem}.png"
1061	        diff_path = self.comparisons_dir / diff_name
1062	
1063	        # Amplify diff for visibility
1064	        amplified = diff.point(lambda x: min(255, x * 3))
1065	        amplified.save(diff_path)
1066	
1067	        # Calculate diff metrics
1068	        total_pixels = img_before.size[0] * img_before.size[1]
1069	        diff_pixels = sum(1 for px in diff.getdata() if sum(px) > 15)
1070	        diff_ratio = diff_pixels / total_pixels if total_pixels > 0 else 0
1071	
1072	        return {
1073	            "before": str(before_path),
1074	            "after": str(after_path),
1075	            "diff": str(diff_path),
1076	            "diff_ratio": round(diff_ratio, 4),
1077	            "diff_pixels": diff_pixels,
1078	            "total_pixels": total_pixels,
1079	            "has_changes": diff_ratio > 0.001,
1080	        }
1081	
1082	    def compare_all(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
1083	        """Compare all matching before/after screenshots."""
1084	        before_dir = self.output_dir / "before"
1085	        after_dir = self.output_dir / "after"
1086	        results = []
1087	
1088	        if not before_dir.exists() or not after_dir.exists():
1089	            log.warning("Missing before/ or after/ directory for comparison")
1090	            return results
1091	
1092	        for before_file in sorted(before_dir.glob("*.png")):
1093	            after_file = after_dir / before_file.name
1094	            result = self.compare(before_file, after_file)
1095	            results.append(result)
1096	            status = "CHANGED" if result.get("has_changes") else "SAME"
1097	            log.info("  %s: %s (%.2f%% diff)", status, before_file.name,
1098	                     result.get("diff_ratio", 0) * 100)
1099	
1100	        return results
1101

1104	# REPORT GENERATOR — JSON + HTML output
