1106	
1107	class ReportGenerator:
1108	    """Generate machine-readable JSON and human-readable HTML audit reports."""
1109	
1110	    def __init__(self, output_dir: Path):
1111	        self.output_dir = output_dir
1112	        self.report_dir = output_dir / "report"
1113	        self.report_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1114	
1115	    def generate(self, heuristic_results: dict[str, list[HeuristicResult]],
1116	                 comparison_results: list[dict[str, Any]],
1117	                 screenshot_count: int,
1118	                 duration_seconds: float) -> tuple[Path, Path]:
1119	        """Generate JSON and HTML reports. Returns (json_path, html_path)."""
1120	        timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
1121	
1122	        # Flatten heuristics
1123	        all_heuristics = []
1124	        for key, results in heuristic_results.items():
1125	            for r in results:
1126	                all_heuristics.append({
1127	                    "state_viewport": key,
1128	                    **asdict(r),
1129	                })
1130	
1131	        # Aggregate pass/fail
1132	        total_checks = len(all_heuristics)
1133	        passed_checks = sum(1 for h in all_heuristics if h["passed"])
1134	
1135	        report_data = {
1136	            "timestamp": timestamp,
1137	            "duration_seconds": round(duration_seconds, 1),
1138	            "screenshot_count": screenshot_count,
1139	            "heuristics": {
1140	                "total": total_checks,
1141	                "passed": passed_checks,
1142	                "failed": total_checks - passed_checks,
1143	                "pass_rate": round(passed_checks / max(total_checks, 1), 3),
1144	                "details": all_heuristics,
1145	            },
1146	            "comparisons": {
1147	                "total": len(comparison_results),
1148	                "changed": sum(1 for c in comparison_results if c.get("has_changes")),
1149	                "details": comparison_results,
1150	            },
1151	        }
1152	
1153	        # Write JSON
1154	        json_path = self.report_dir / "design_audit_report.json"
1155	        with open(json_path, "w") as f:
1156	            json.dump(report_data, f, indent=2)
1157	        log.info("JSON report: %s", json_path)
1158	
1159	        # Write HTML
1160	        html_path = self.report_dir / "design_audit_report.html"
1161	        html = self._render_html(report_data)
1162	        with open(html_path, "w") as f:
1163	            f.write(html)
1164	        log.info("HTML report: %s", html_path)
1165	
1166	        return json_path, html_path
1167	
1168	    def _render_html(self, data: dict) -> str:
1169	        """Render an HTML report with embedded thumbnails."""
1170	        h = data["heuristics"]
1171	        c = data["comparisons"]
1172	
1173	        # Heuristic summary rows
1174	        heuristic_rows = ""
1175	        for detail in h["details"]:
1176	            status = "PASS" if detail["passed"] else "FAIL"
1177	            bg = "#1a3a1a" if detail["passed"] else "#3a1a1a"
1178	            color = "#34d399" if detail["passed"] else "#fb7185"
1179	            violations_str = ""
1180	            if detail["violations"]:
1181	                violations_str = f' <span style="color:#a0a0ab">({len(detail["violations"])} violations)</span>'
1182	            heuristic_rows += f"""
1183	            <tr style="background:{bg}">
1184	                <td style="color:{color};font-weight:700">{status}</td>
1185	                <td>{detail["id"]}</td>
1186	                <td>{detail["name"]}{violations_str}</td>
1187	                <td>{detail["state_viewport"]}</td>
1188	                <td>{detail["score"]:.0%}</td>
1189	                <td>{detail["threshold"]:.0%}</td>
1190	            </tr>"""
1191	
1192	        # Comparison rows
1193	        comparison_rows = ""
1194	        for comp in c["details"]:
1195	            if comp.get("error"):
1196	                comparison_rows += f"""
1197	                <tr style="background:#3a3a1a">
1198	                    <td colspan="4">ERROR: {comp["error"]}</td>
1199	                </tr>"""
1200	                continue
1201	            status = "CHANGED" if comp.get("has_changes") else "SAME"
1202	            bg = "#1a2a3a" if comp.get("has_changes") else "#1a1a1a"
1203	            before_name = Path(comp["before"]).name if comp.get("before") else "N/A"
1204	            comparison_rows += f"""
1205	            <tr style="background:{bg}">
1206	                <td>{status}</td>
1207	                <td>{before_name}</td>
1208	                <td>{comp.get("diff_ratio", 0):.2%}</td>
1209	                <td>{comp.get("diff_pixels", 0):,}</td>
1210	            </tr>"""
1211	
1212	        return f"""<!DOCTYPE html>
1213	<html lang="en">
1214	<head>
1215	<meta charset="utf-8">
1216	<title>POLARIS Design Audit Report</title>
1217	<style>
1218	    body {{ font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; background: #0c0c0f; color: #fafaf9; padding: 40px; max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; }}
1219	    h1 {{ font-size: 1.5rem; letter-spacing: 2px; color: #38bdf8; border-bottom: 1px solid #2a2a30; padding-bottom: 12px; }}
1220	    h2 {{ font-size: 1.1rem; color: #a0a0ab; margin-top: 32px; }}
1221	    .summary {{ display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 12px; margin: 20px 0; }}
1222	    .stat {{ background: #19191d; border: 1px solid #2a2a30; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; text-align: center; }}
1223	    .stat .val {{ font-size: 1.75rem; font-weight: 700; font-family: monospace; }}
1224	    .stat .lbl {{ font-size: 0.75rem; color: #6e6e7a; margin-top: 4px; }}
1225	    .pass {{ color: #34d399; }}
1226	    .fail {{ color: #fb7185; }}
1227	    table {{ width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 0.8125rem; margin: 12px 0; }}
1228	    th {{ text-align: left; padding: 8px 12px; background: #111114; color: #6e6e7a; font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.5px; font-size: 0.6875rem; }}
1229	    td {{ padding: 8px 12px; border-bottom: 1px solid #1f1f24; }}
1230	    .ts {{ font-size: 0.6875rem; color: #6e6e7a; }}
1231	</style>
1232	</head>
1233	<body>
1234	<h1>POLARIS DESIGN AUDIT REPORT</h1>
1235	<p class="ts">Generated: {data["timestamp"]} | Duration: {data["duration_seconds"]}s | Screenshots: {data["screenshot_count"]}</p>
1236	
1237	<div class="summary">
1238	    <div class="stat"><div class="val {'pass' if h['pass_rate'] >= 0.9 else 'fail'}">{h['pass_rate']:.0%}</div><div class="lbl">Heuristic Pass Rate</div></div>
1239	    <div class="stat"><div class="val pass">{h['passed']}</div><div class="lbl">Checks Passed</div></div>
1240	    <div class="stat"><div class="val fail">{h['failed']}</div><div class="lbl">Checks Failed</div></div>
1241	    <div class="stat"><div class="val">{c['changed']}/{c['total']}</div><div class="lbl">Screenshots Changed</div></div>
1242	</div>
1243	
1244	<h2>Heuristic Results</h2>
1245	<table>
1246	<thead><tr><th>Status</th><th>ID</th><th>Check</th><th>State/Viewport</th><th>Score</th><th>Threshold</th></tr></thead>
1247	<tbody>{heuristic_rows}</tbody>
1248	</table>
1249	
1250	<h2>Before/After Comparisons</h2>
1251	<table>
1252	<thead><tr><th>Status</th><th>Screenshot</th><th>Diff Ratio</th><th>Diff Pixels</th></tr></thead>
1253	<tbody>{comparison_rows if comparison_rows else '<tr><td colspan="4">No comparisons available. Run --phase compare after capturing before and after.</td></tr>'}</tbody>
1254	</table>
1255	</body>
1256	</html>"""

1259	# =========================================================================
1260	# MAIN ORCHESTRATOR
1261	# =========================================================================
1262	
1263	class DesignAuditRunner:
1264	    """Main orchestrator for the design audit pipeline."""
1265	
1266	    def __init__(self, args: argparse.Namespace):
1267	        self.args = args
1268	        self.port = args.port
1269	        self.phase = args.phase
1270	        self.headless = args.headless
1271	        self.output_dir = Path(args.output)
1272	        self.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1273	
1274	        # Parse filters
1275	        self.selected_states = self._parse_states(args.states)
1276	        self.selected_viewports = self._parse_viewports(args.viewports)
1277	        self.selected_themes = self._parse_themes(args.themes)
1278	
1279	        # Components
1280	        self.server = ServerLifecycle(self.port)
1281	        self.navigator = StateNavigator(self.port)
1282	        self.capture = ScreenshotCapture(self.output_dir)
1283	        self.heuristics = HeuristicEngine()
1284	        self.comparisons = ComparisonEngine(self.output_dir)
1285	        self.reporter = ReportGenerator(self.output_dir)
1286	
1287	    def _parse_states(self, states_arg: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
1288	        if states_arg == "all":
1289	            return STATE_MATRIX
1290	        ids = [s.strip().upper() for s in states_arg.split(",")]
1291	        return [s for s in STATE_MATRIX if s["id"] in ids]
1292	
1293	    def _parse_viewports(self, vp_arg: str) -> dict[str, dict[str, int]]:
1294	        if vp_arg == "all":
1295	            return VIEWPORTS
1296	        names = [v.strip() for v in vp_arg.split(",")]
1297	        return {k: v for k, v in VIEWPORTS.items() if k in names}
1298	
1299	    def _parse_themes(self, themes_arg: str) -> list[str]:
1300	        if themes_arg == "both":
1301	            return THEMES
1302	        return [themes_arg]
1303	
1304	    async def run(self) -> int:
1305	        """Execute the audit pipeline. Returns 0 on pass, 1 on fail."""
1306	        start_time = time.time()
1307	        screenshot_count = 0
1308	        heuristic_results: dict[str, list[HeuristicResult]] = {}
1309	        comparison_results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
1310	
1311	        try:
1312	            await self.server.start()
1313	
1314	            async with async_playwright() as p:
1315	                browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=self.headless)
1316	
1317	                for vp_name, vp_size in self.selected_viewports.items():
1318	                    for theme in self.selected_themes:
1319	                        context = await browser.new_context(
1320	                            viewport=vp_size,
1321	                            device_scale_factor=1,
1322	                            color_scheme="dark" if theme == "dark" else "light",
1323	                        )
1324	                        page = await context.new_page()
1325	                        await self.navigator.setup_page(page)
1326	
1327	                        for state in self.selected_states:
1328	                            state_label = f"{state['id']}:{state['name']}"
1329	                            log.info("[%s] %s / %s / %s", self.phase, state_label, theme, vp_name)
1330	
1331	                            try:
1332	                                await self.navigator.navigate_to_state(page, state, theme)
1333	                            except Exception as exc:
1334	                                log.error("  Navigation failed: %s", exc)
1335	                                continue
1336	
1337	                            # Screenshot phases
1338	                            if self.phase in ("before", "after", "full"):
1339	                                await self.capture.capture(
1340	                                    page, state["name"], theme, vp_name, self.phase
1341	                                    if self.phase != "full" else "after"
1342	                                )
1343	                                screenshot_count += 1
1344	
1345	                            # Heuristic phase
1346	                            if self.phase in ("heuristics", "full"):
1347	                                key = f"{state['name']}_{theme}_{vp_name}"
1348	                                results = await self.heuristics.run_all(page, vp_name)
1349	                                heuristic_results[key] = results
1350	
1351	                        await context.close()
1352	
1353	                await browser.close()
1354	
1355	            # Comparison phase
1356	            if self.phase in ("compare", "full"):
1357	                comparison_results = self.comparisons.compare_all()
1358	
1359	            # Generate report
1360	            duration = time.time() - start_time
1361	            json_path, html_path = self.reporter.generate(
1362	                heuristic_results, comparison_results, screenshot_count, duration
1363	            )
1364	
1365	            # Summary
1366	            total_h = sum(len(v) for v in heuristic_results.values())
1367	            passed_h = sum(1 for v in heuristic_results.values() for r in v if r.passed)
1368	            failed_h = total_h - passed_h
1369	
1370	            log.info("=" * 60)
1371	            log.info("DESIGN AUDIT COMPLETE")
1372	            log.info("  Phase: %s", self.phase)
1373	            log.info("  Screenshots: %d", screenshot_count)
1374	            log.info("  Heuristics: %d passed, %d failed (of %d)", passed_h, failed_h, total_h)
1375	            log.info("  Comparisons: %d files", len(comparison_results))
1376	            log.info("  Duration: %.1fs", duration)
1377	            log.info("  Report: %s", html_path)
1378	            log.info("=" * 60)
1379	
1380	            return 0 if failed_h == 0 else 1
1381	
1382	        except Exception as exc:
1383	            log.error("Audit failed: %s", exc, exc_info=True)
1384	            return 1
1385	        finally:
1386	            await self.server.stop()
1387	
1388

1390	# CLI
1391	# =========================================================================
1392	
1393	def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
1394	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
1395	        description="POLARIS Design Audit — Exhaustive Visual QA System"
1396	    )
1397	    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=DEFAULT_PORT,
1398	                        help=f"Server port (default: {DEFAULT_PORT})")
1399	    parser.add_argument("--phase", choices=["before", "after", "compare", "heuristics", "full"],
1400	                        default="full", help="Audit phase to run")
1401	    parser.add_argument("--states", default="all",
1402	                        help="Comma-separated state IDs (e.g. S06,S07) or 'all'")
1403	    parser.add_argument("--viewports", default="all",
1404	                        help="Comma-separated viewport names or 'all'")
1405	    parser.add_argument("--themes", choices=["dark", "light", "both"], default="both",
1406	                        help="Theme(s) to test")
1407	    parser.add_argument("--output", default=str(OUTPUT_DIR),
1408	                        help=f"Output directory (default: {OUTPUT_DIR})")
1409	    parser.add_argument("--headless", action="store_true", default=True,
1410	                        help="Run headless (default: true)")
1411	    parser.add_argument("--no-headless", dest="headless", action="store_false",
1412	                        help="Run with visible browser")
1413	    return parser.parse_args()
1414	
1415	
1416	async def main() -> int:
1417	    args = parse_args()
1418	    runner = DesignAuditRunner(args)
1419	    return await runner.run()
1420	
1421	
1422	if __name__ == "__main__":
1423	    exit_code = asyncio.run(main())
1424	    sys.exit(exit_code)

1	"""
2	Exhaustive Playwright-based UI audit for the POLARIS dashboard.
3	
4	Runs 79 checks across 19 categories (A-S) covering connectivity, navigation,
5	metrics, evidence, report, citation chain, advanced views, API endpoints,
6	visual integrity, and real-time updates.
7	
8	Usage:
9	    python scripts/playwright_fire_test.py [--port PORT] [--output-dir DIR]
10	                                           [--trace TRACE] [--headed]
11	
12	Requirements:
13	    - playwright (async_api)
14	    - scripts/live_server.py (FastAPI server)
15	    - A valid JSONL trace file (default: logs/pg_trace_SHOWME_TEST_002.jsonl)
16	"""
17	
18	import argparse
19	import asyncio
20	import json
21	import logging
22	import os
23	import re
24	import socket
25	import subprocess
26	import sys
27	import time
28	from datetime import datetime, timezone
29	from pathlib import Path
30	from typing import Any
31	
32	from playwright.async_api import async_playwright, Page
33	
34	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	# Project root (LAW VI -- no hard-coded paths)
36	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
37	_PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
38	_SCRIPTS_DIR = _PROJECT_ROOT / "scripts"
39	_LIVE_SERVER_SCRIPT = _SCRIPTS_DIR / "live_server.py"
40	_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR = _PROJECT_ROOT / "outputs" / "fire_test"
41	_TRACE_FILE = _PROJECT_ROOT / "logs" / "pg_trace_SHOWME_TEST_002.jsonl"
42	
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	# Timing constants (LAW VI -- configurable via env)
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	SERVER_READY_TIMEOUT_S = int(os.getenv("PW_SERVER_READY_TIMEOUT", "30"))
47	SERVER_POLL_INTERVAL_S = float(os.getenv("PW_SERVER_POLL_INTERVAL", "0.5"))
48	HYDRATION_TIMEOUT_S = int(os.getenv("PW_HYDRATION_TIMEOUT", "60"))
49	HYDRATION_TARGET = int(os.getenv("PW_HYDRATION_TARGET", "800"))
50	PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT_MS = int(os.getenv("PW_PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT_MS", "60000"))
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# View names for navigation
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	ALL_VIEWS = [
56	    "campaigns",
57	    "research",
58	    "evidence",
59	    "report",
60	    "memory",
61	    "pipelines",
62	    "advanced",
63	]
64	
65	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	# Logging
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	logging.basicConfig(
69	    level=logging.INFO,
70	    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(message)s",
71	    datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
72	)
73	logger = logging.getLogger("fire_test")
74	
75	
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	# Utility: find a free TCP port
78	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
79	def find_free_port() -> int:
80	    """Bind to port 0 and let the OS assign a free port."""
81	    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as sock:
82	        sock.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
83	        return sock.getsockname()[1]
84	
85	
86	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
87	# Utility: wait for server readiness
88	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
89	async def wait_for_server(port: int, timeout_s: int) -> bool:
90	    """Poll the server TCP port until it accepts connections or timeout."""
91	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s
92	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
93	        try:
94	            reader, writer = await asyncio.open_connection("127.0.0.1", port)
95	            writer.close()
96	            await writer.wait_closed()
97	            return True
98	        except (ConnectionRefusedError, OSError):
99	            await asyncio.sleep(SERVER_POLL_INTERVAL_S)
100	    return False
101	
102	
103	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
104	# Server lifecycle
105	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
106	def start_server(port: int, trace_file: str, log_dir: Path) -> subprocess.Popen:
107	    """
108	    Start live_server.py on the given port with --trace and --no-tunnel.
109	
110	    CRITICAL: stdout/stderr redirected to a log file to avoid
111	    subprocess.PIPE deadlocks on Windows (MEMORY lesson #16).
112	    """
113	    log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
114	    server_log_path = log_dir / "fire_test_server.log"
115	    server_log_handle = open(server_log_path, "w", encoding="utf-8")
116	
117	    cmd = [
118	        sys.executable,
119	        str(_LIVE_SERVER_SCRIPT),
120	        "--port", str(port),
121	        "--trace", str(trace_file),
122	        "--no-tunnel",
123	    ]
124	    logger.info("Starting server: %s", " ".join(cmd))
125	    logger.info("Server log: %s", server_log_path)
126	
127	    proc = subprocess.Popen(
128	        cmd,
129	        stdout=server_log_handle,
130	        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
131	        cwd=str(_PROJECT_ROOT),
132	    )
133	    # Keep handle reference so it stays open while server runs
134	    proc._log_handle = server_log_handle  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
135	    return proc
136	
137	
138	def stop_server(proc: subprocess.Popen) -> None:
139	    """Terminate the server process and close the log handle."""
140	    if proc.poll() is None:
141	        logger.info("Terminating server (pid=%d)", proc.pid)
142	        proc.terminate()
143	        try:
144	            proc.wait(timeout=5)
145	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
146	            proc.kill()
147	            proc.wait(timeout=3)
148	    log_handle = getattr(proc, "_log_handle", None)
149	    if log_handle and not log_handle.closed:
150	        log_handle.close()
151	
152	
153	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
154	# CheckResult
155	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
156	class CheckResult:
157	    """Container for a single audit check result."""
158	
159	    def __init__(self, check_id: str, description: str, category: str):
160	        self.check_id = check_id
161	        self.description = description
162	        self.category = category
163	        self.passed: bool = False
164	        self.severity: str = "error"  # error, warning, info
165	        self.actual: Any = None
166	        self.expected: Any = None
167	        self.error: str | None = None
168	        self.screenshot: str | None = None
169	
170	    def set_pass(self, actual: Any = None, expected: Any = None) -> "CheckResult":
171	        self.passed = True
172	        self.actual = actual
173	        self.expected = expected
174	        return self
175	
176	    def set_fail(
177	        self,
178	        actual: Any = None,
179	        expected: Any = None,
180	        error: str | None = None,
181	    ) -> "CheckResult":
182	        self.passed = False
183	        self.actual = actual
184	        self.expected = expected
185	        self.error = error
186	        return self
187	
188	    def set_info(self, actual: Any = None, note: str | None = None) -> "CheckResult":
189	        """For known bugs -- document state without failing."""
190	        self.passed = True
191	        self.severity = "info"
192	        self.actual = actual
193	        self.error = note
194	        return self
195	
196	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
197	        result: dict[str, Any] = {
198	            "check_id": self.check_id,
199	            "description": self.description,
200	            "category": self.category,
201	            "passed": self.passed,
202	            "severity": self.severity,
203	            "actual": self.actual,
204	            "expected": self.expected,
205	        }
206	        if self.error:
207	            result["error"] = self.error
208	        if self.screenshot:
209	            result["screenshot"] = self.screenshot
210	        return result
211	
212	
213	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	# DOM / Page helpers
215	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
216	async def element_count(page: Page, selector: str) -> int:
217	    """Return count of elements matching selector."""
218	    return await page.evaluate(
219	        "(sel) => document.querySelectorAll(sel).length", selector
220	    )
221	
222	
223	async def element_text(page: Page, selector: str) -> str:
224	    """Return textContent of first matching element."""
225	    return await page.evaluate(
226	        """
227	        (sel) => {
228	            const el = document.querySelector(sel);
229	            return el ? el.textContent.trim() : '__NOT_FOUND__';
230	        }
231	        """,
232	        selector,
233	    )
234	
235	
236	async def element_exists(page: Page, selector: str) -> bool:
237	    """Return True if at least one element matches the selector."""
238	    return await page.evaluate(
239	        "(sel) => document.querySelector(sel) !== null", selector
240	    )
241	
242	
243	async def element_inner_html_len(page: Page, selector: str) -> int:
244	    """Return the length of innerHTML for the first matching element."""
245	    return await page.evaluate(
246	        """
247	        (sel) => {
248	            const el = document.querySelector(sel);
249	            return el ? el.innerHTML.length : 0;
250	        }
251	        """,
252	        selector,
253	    )
254	
255	
256	async def get_computed_style(page: Page, selector: str, prop: str) -> str:
257	    """Return a computed CSS property value for the first matching element."""
258	    return await page.evaluate(
259	        """
260	        ([sel, prop]) => {
261	            const el = document.querySelector(sel);
262	            if (!el) return '__NOT_FOUND__';
263	            return window.getComputedStyle(el)[prop];
264	        }
265	        """,
266	        [selector, prop],
267	    )
268	
269	
270	async def wait_for_hydration(page: Page, target: int, timeout_s: int) -> bool:
271	    """Poll state.eventCount until it reaches target or timeout expires."""
272	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s
273	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
274	        count = await page.evaluate(
275	            "() => { try { return state.eventCount || 0; } catch(e) { return 0; } }"
276	        )
277	        if count >= target:
278	            return True
279	        await asyncio.sleep(0.5)
280	    return False
281	
282	
283	async def switch_to_operator_mode(page: Page) -> None:
284	    """Switch dashboard to operator mode."""
285	    await page.evaluate("setViewMode('operator')")
286	    await page.wait_for_timeout(300)
287	
288	
289	async def switch_to_user_mode(page: Page) -> None:
290	    """Switch dashboard to user mode."""
291	    await page.evaluate("setViewMode('user')")
292	    await page.wait_for_timeout(300)

294	
295	async def switch_view(page: Page, view_name: str) -> None:
296	    """Switch to the named view and wait for rendering."""
297	    await page.evaluate(f"switchView('{view_name}')")
298	    await page.wait_for_timeout(500)

301	async def capture_view_screenshot(
302	    page: Page, view_name: str, output_dir: Path
303	) -> str:
304	    """Take a screenshot of the current view and return the file path."""
305	    screenshots_dir = output_dir / "screenshots"
306	    screenshots_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

309	    logger.info("Screenshot: %s", file_path)

312	
313	async def capture_bug_screenshot(
314	    page: Page, check_id: str, output_dir: Path
315	) -> str:
316	    """Take a screenshot for a failing check and return the file path."""
317	    screenshots_dir = output_dir / "screenshots"
318	    screenshots_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
319	    file_path = screenshots_dir / f"bug_{check_id}.png"
320	    await page.screenshot(path=str(file_path), full_page=True)
321	    logger.info("Bug screenshot: %s", file_path)

324	
325	async def api_fetch(page: Page, path: str) -> dict:
326	    """Execute a fetch() from inside the browser and return status info."""
327	    return await page.evaluate(
328	        """
329	        (path) => fetch(path)
330	            .then(r => ({status: r.status, ok: r.ok}))
331	            .catch(e => ({status: 0, ok: false, error: e.message}))
332	        """,
333	        path,
334	    )
335	
336	
337	async def api_fetch_json(page: Page, path: str) -> dict:
338	    """Execute a fetch() and return the parsed JSON body alongside status."""
339	    return await page.evaluate(
340	        """
341	        (path) => fetch(path)
342	            .then(async r => {
343	                const body = await r.json().catch(() => null);
344	                return {status: r.status, ok: r.ok, body: body};
345	            })
346	            .catch(e => ({status: 0, ok: false, error: e.message, body: null}))
347	        """,
348	        path,
349	    )
350	
351	
352	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
353	# Category A: Connectivity & Hydration (A01-A06)
354	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
355	async def run_connectivity_checks(page: Page) -> list[CheckResult]:
356	    """A01-A06: Connectivity and hydration checks."""
357	    results: list[CheckResult] = []
358	
359	    # A01: Page title contains "POLARIS"
360	    a01 = CheckResult("A01", "Page title contains 'POLARIS'", "A. Connectivity & Hydration")
361	    title = await page.title()
362	    if "POLARIS" in title.upper():
363	        a01.set_pass(actual=title, expected="contains 'POLARIS'")
364	    else:
365	        a01.set_fail(actual=title, expected="contains 'POLARIS'", error=f"Title is '{title}'")
366	    results.append(a01)
367	
368	    # A02: /api/snapshot returns JSON with "total_event_count"
369	    a02 = CheckResult("A02", "/api/snapshot returns JSON with total_event_count", "A. Connectivity & Hydration")
370	    snapshot = await api_fetch_json(page, "/api/snapshot")
371	    if snapshot.get("ok") and snapshot.get("body") and "total_event_count" in (snapshot.get("body") or {}):
372	        a02.set_pass(
373	            actual={"status": snapshot["status"], "total_event_count": snapshot["body"]["total_event_count"]},
374	            expected="status 200 with total_event_count",
375	        )
376	    else:
377	        a02.set_fail(
378	            actual=snapshot,
379	            expected="status 200 with total_event_count",
380	            error="Snapshot missing or no total_event_count key",
381	        )
382	    results.append(a02)
383	
384	    # A03: #status-dot has class "connected" or "completed" (completed after pipeline finishes)
385	    a03 = CheckResult("A03", "#status-dot has class 'connected' or 'completed'", "A. Connectivity & Hydration")
386	    dot_class = await page.evaluate(
387	        "() => { const el = document.getElementById('status-dot'); return el ? el.className : '__NOT_FOUND__'; }"
388	    )
389	    if "connected" in str(dot_class) or "completed" in str(dot_class):
390	        a03.set_pass(actual=dot_class, expected="contains 'connected' or 'completed'")
391	    else:
392	        a03.set_fail(actual=dot_class, expected="contains 'connected' or 'completed'", error=f"className is '{dot_class}'")
393	    results.append(a03)
394	
395	    # A04: state.eventCount > 800
396	    a04 = CheckResult("A04", "state.eventCount > 800", "A. Connectivity & Hydration")
397	    event_count = await page.evaluate(
398	        "() => { try { return state.eventCount || 0; } catch(e) { return 0; } }"
399	    )
400	    if event_count > 800:
401	        a04.set_pass(actual=event_count, expected="> 800")
402	    else:
403	        a04.set_fail(actual=event_count, expected="> 800", error=f"eventCount is {event_count}")
404	    results.append(a04)
405	
406	    # A05: state.vectorId is not "--" and not empty
407	    a05 = CheckResult("A05", "state.vectorId is a valid identifier", "A. Connectivity & Hydration")
408	    vector_id = await page.evaluate(
409	        "() => { try { return state.vectorId || ''; } catch(e) { return ''; } }"
410	    )
411	    if vector_id and vector_id != "--" and vector_id.strip():
412	        a05.set_pass(actual=vector_id, expected="non-empty, not '--'")
413	    else:
414	        a05.set_fail(actual=vector_id, expected="non-empty, not '--'", error=f"vectorId is '{vector_id}'")
415	    results.append(a05)
416	
417	    # A06: state.researchQuery is non-empty string
418	    a06 = CheckResult("A06", "state.researchQuery is non-empty", "A. Connectivity & Hydration")
419	    query = await page.evaluate(
420	        "() => { try { return state.researchQuery || ''; } catch(e) { return ''; } }"
421	    )
422	    if query and query.strip():
423	        a06.set_pass(actual=query, expected="non-empty string")
424	    else:
425	        a06.set_fail(actual=query, expected="non-empty string", error=f"researchQuery is '{query}'")
426	    results.append(a06)
427	
428	    return results
429	
430	
431	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
432	# Category B: Timer System (B07-B10)
433	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
434	async def run_timer_checks(page: Page) -> list[CheckResult]:
435	    """B07-B10: Timer display and freeze checks."""
436	    results: list[CheckResult] = []
437	
438	    # B07: #elapsed-time is not "00:00:00"
439	    b07 = CheckResult("B07", "#elapsed-time is not '00:00:00'", "B. Timer System")
440	    timer_text = await element_text(page, "#elapsed-time")
441	    if timer_text != "00:00:00" and timer_text != "__NOT_FOUND__":
442	        b07.set_pass(actual=timer_text, expected="not '00:00:00'")
443	    else:
444	        b07.set_fail(actual=timer_text, expected="not '00:00:00'", error=f"Timer shows '{timer_text}'")
445	    results.append(b07)
446	
447	    # B08: Timer text matches HH:MM:SS regex
448	    b08 = CheckResult("B08", "Timer text matches HH:MM:SS format", "B. Timer System")
449	    if re.match(r"\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}", timer_text):
450	        b08.set_pass(actual=timer_text, expected=r"\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}")
451	    else:
452	        b08.set_fail(actual=timer_text, expected=r"\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}", error=f"Timer text '{timer_text}' doesn't match")
453	    results.append(b08)
454	
455	    # B09: Parsed seconds > 3600 (pipeline ran ~2 hours)
456	    b09 = CheckResult("B09", "Timer value > 3600s (pipeline ran > 1 hour)", "B. Timer System")
457	    total_seconds = _parse_hms_to_seconds(timer_text)
458	    if total_seconds > 3600:
459	        b09.set_pass(actual=f"{total_seconds}s", expected="> 3600s")
460	    else:
461	        b09.set_fail(actual=f"{total_seconds}s", expected="> 3600s", error=f"Only {total_seconds}s elapsed")
462	    results.append(b09)
463	
464	    # B10: Timer is frozen (read twice with 2s gap, should be identical)
465	    b10 = CheckResult("B10", "Timer is frozen (identical readings 2s apart)", "B. Timer System")
466	    reading_1 = await element_text(page, "#elapsed-time")
467	    await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
468	    reading_2 = await element_text(page, "#elapsed-time")
469	    if reading_1 == reading_2:
470	        b10.set_pass(actual=f"{reading_1} == {reading_2}", expected="identical readings")
471	    else:
472	        b10.set_fail(
473	            actual=f"{reading_1} != {reading_2}",
474	            expected="identical readings",
475	            error="Timer is still ticking",
476	        )
477	    results.append(b10)
478	
479	    return results
480	
481	
482	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
483	# Category C: Navigation & View Switching (C11-C15)
484	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
485	async def run_navigation_checks(page: Page) -> list[CheckResult]:
486	    """C11-C15: Navigation buttons and view switching."""
487	    results: list[CheckResult] = []
488	
489	    # C11: At least 7 .nav-btn elements
490	    c11 = CheckResult("C11", "At least 7 .nav-btn elements", "C. Navigation & View Switching")
491	    nav_count = await element_count(page, ".nav-btn")
492	    if nav_count >= 7:
493	        c11.set_pass(actual=nav_count, expected=">= 7")
494	    else:
495	        c11.set_fail(actual=nav_count, expected=">= 7", error=f"Found {nav_count} nav buttons")
496	    results.append(c11)
497	
498	    # C12: Each view activates correctly
499	    c12 = CheckResult("C12", "All 7 views activate via switchView()", "C. Navigation & View Switching")
500	    failed_views: list[str] = []
501	    for view_name in ALL_VIEWS:
502	        await switch_view(page, view_name)
503	        is_active = await page.evaluate(
504	            f"document.querySelector('#view-{view_name}') !== null && "
505	            f"document.querySelector('#view-{view_name}').classList.contains('active')"
506	        )
507	        if not is_active:
508	            failed_views.append(view_name)
509	    if not failed_views:
510	        c12.set_pass(actual="all 7 views activate", expected="all views active")
511	    else:
512	        c12.set_fail(
513	            actual=f"failed: {failed_views}",
514	            expected="all views active",
515	            error=f"Views that did not activate: {failed_views}",
516	        )
517	    results.append(c12)
518	
519	    # C13: Advanced view has >= 5 tab buttons, each activating its pane
520	    c13 = CheckResult("C13", "Advanced view has >= 5 tabs, each activating its pane", "C. Navigation & View Switching")
521	    await switch_view(page, "advanced")
522	    adv_tab_count = await element_count(page, ".adv-tab-btn")
523	    if adv_tab_count >= 5:
524	        # Click each tab and verify a corresponding .adv-pane.active appears
525	        failed_tabs: list[int] = []
526	        for idx in range(adv_tab_count):
527	            await page.evaluate(
528	                f"document.querySelectorAll('.adv-tab-btn')[{idx}].click()"
529	            )
530	            await page.wait_for_timeout(300)
531	            has_active_pane = await page.evaluate(
532	                "document.querySelector('.adv-pane.active') !== null"
533	            )
534	            if not has_active_pane:
535	                failed_tabs.append(idx)
536	        if not failed_tabs:
537	            c13.set_pass(actual=f"{adv_tab_count} tabs, all panes activate", expected=">= 5 tabs, all panes")
538	        else:
539	            c13.set_fail(
540	                actual=f"{adv_tab_count} tabs, failed pane indices: {failed_tabs}",
541	                expected=">= 5 tabs, all panes",
542	                error=f"Tabs at indices {failed_tabs} did not activate pane",
543	            )
544	    else:
545	        c13.set_fail(
546	            actual=adv_tab_count,
547	            expected=">= 5",
548	            error=f"Only {adv_tab_count} advanced tab buttons found",
549	        )
550	    results.append(c13)
551	
552	    # C14: User mode toggle works
553	    c14 = CheckResult("C14", "setViewMode('user') / setViewMode('operator') toggle body class", "C. Navigation & View Switching")
554	    await page.evaluate("setViewMode('user')")
555	    await page.wait_for_timeout(300)
556	    has_user_class = await page.evaluate(
557	        "document.body.classList.contains('user-mode')"
558	    )
559	    await page.evaluate("setViewMode('operator')")
560	    await page.wait_for_timeout(300)
561	    no_user_class = await page.evaluate(
562	        "!document.body.classList.contains('user-mode')"
563	    )
564	    if has_user_class and no_user_class:
565	        c14.set_pass(actual="user-mode toggles correctly", expected="class toggles")
566	    else:
567	        c14.set_fail(
568	            actual=f"user_mode_set={has_user_class}, operator_cleared={no_user_class}",
569	            expected="both true",
570	            error="Body class toggle failed",
571	        )
572	    results.append(c14)
573	
574	    # C15: Theme toggle changes data-theme attribute
575	    c15 = CheckResult("C15", "toggleTheme() changes data-theme attribute", "C. Navigation & View Switching")
576	    theme_before = await page.evaluate(
577	        "document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') || 'none'"
578	    )
579	    await page.evaluate("toggleTheme()")
580	    await page.wait_for_timeout(300)
581	    theme_after = await page.evaluate(
582	        "document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') || 'none'"
583	    )
584	    if theme_before != theme_after:
585	        c15.set_pass(actual=f"{theme_before} -> {theme_after}", expected="theme changes")
586	    else:
587	        c15.set_fail(
588	            actual=f"before={theme_before}, after={theme_after}",
589	            expected="different themes",
590	            error="data-theme did not change",
591	        )
592	    # Toggle back to original theme
593	    await page.evaluate("toggleTheme()")
594	    await page.wait_for_timeout(200)
595	    results.append(c15)
596	
597	    return results

699	            const items = document.querySelectorAll('.step-item');

content.text: Let me continue reading the rest of this large file in chunks.

content.content:
700	            const bad = [];

1647	async def run_api_smoke_checks(page: Page) -> list[CheckResult]:
1648	    """Q67-Q72: API endpoint availability."""
1649	    results: list[CheckResult] = []
1650	
1651	    # Q67: /api/snapshot -> 200
1652	    q67 = CheckResult("Q67", "/api/snapshot returns 200", "Q. API Smoke Tests")
1653	    resp = await api_fetch(page, "/api/snapshot")
1654	    if resp.get("ok"):
1655	        q67.set_pass(actual=resp.get("status"), expected=200)
1656	    else:
1657	        q67.set_fail(actual=resp, expected=200, error=f"Status {resp.get('status')}")
1658	    results.append(q67)
1659	
1660	    # Q68: /api/cost -> 200
1661	    q68 = CheckResult("Q68", "/api/cost returns 200", "Q. API Smoke Tests")
1662	    resp = await api_fetch(page, "/api/cost")
1663	    if resp.get("ok"):
1664	        q68.set_pass(actual=resp.get("status"), expected=200)
1665	    else:
1666	        q68.set_fail(actual=resp, expected=200, error=f"Status {resp.get('status')}")
1667	    results.append(q68)
1668	
1669	    # Q69: /api/anomalies -> 200
1670	    q69 = CheckResult("Q69", "/api/anomalies returns 200", "Q. API Smoke Tests")
1671	    resp = await api_fetch(page, "/api/anomalies")
1672	    if resp.get("ok"):
1673	        q69.set_pass(actual=resp.get("status"), expected=200)
1674	    else:
1675	        q69.set_fail(actual=resp, expected=200, error=f"Status {resp.get('status')}")
1676	    results.append(q69)
1677	
1678	    # Q70: /api/research/result/{vectorId} -> 200
1679	    q70 = CheckResult("Q70", "/api/research/result/{vectorId} returns 200", "Q. API Smoke Tests")
1680	    vector_id = await page.evaluate(
1681	        "() => { try { return state.vectorId || ''; } catch(e) { return ''; } }"
1682	    )
1683	    if vector_id and vector_id != "--":
1684	        resp = await api_fetch(page, f"/api/research/result/{vector_id}")
1685	        if resp.get("ok"):
1686	            q70.set_pass(actual=resp.get("status"), expected=200)
1687	        else:
1688	            q70.set_fail(actual=resp, expected=200, error=f"Status {resp.get('status')} for vectorId={vector_id}")
1689	    else:
1690	        q70.set_info(actual=f"vectorId='{vector_id}'", note="Cannot test: no valid vectorId")
1691	    results.append(q70)
1692	
1693	    # Q71: /api/research/chain/{vectorId}/1 -> 200
1694	    q71 = CheckResult("Q71", "/api/research/chain/{vectorId}/1 returns 200", "Q. API Smoke Tests")
1695	    if vector_id and vector_id != "--":
1696	        resp = await api_fetch(page, f"/api/research/chain/{vector_id}/1")
1697	        if resp.get("ok"):
1698	            q71.set_pass(actual=resp.get("status"), expected=200)
1699	        else:
1700	            q71.set_fail(actual=resp, expected=200, error=f"Status {resp.get('status')}")
1701	    else:
1702	        q71.set_info(actual=f"vectorId='{vector_id}'", note="Cannot test: no valid vectorId")
1703	    results.append(q71)
1704	
1705	    # Q72: /api/research/source-preview/{vectorId}/{evidenceId} -> 200 with has_preview
1706	    q72 = CheckResult("Q72", "/api/research/source-preview/{vectorId}/{eid} returns 200 with has_preview", "Q. API Smoke Tests")
1707	    if vector_id and vector_id != "--":
1708	        # Get a valid evidence_id directly from the result JSON (browser state uses trace IDs
1709	        # which may not fully overlap with the final result evidence pool)
1710	        evidence_id = await page.evaluate(
1711	            """
1712	            (vid) => {
1713	                // Try browser-side fetch of the result JSON for a valid evidence_id
1714	                return fetch('/api/research/source-preview/' + vid + '/probe')
1715	                    .catch(() => null)
1716	                    .then(() => {
1717	                        // Since result API doesn't expose evidence IDs, try state.evidenceDetails
1718	                        if (typeof state !== 'undefined' && state.evidenceDetails) {
1719	                            for (var i = 0; i < state.evidenceDetails.length; i++) {
1720	                                var eid = state.evidenceDetails[i].evidence_id || state.evidenceDetails[i].id || '';
1721	                                if (eid) return eid;
1722	                            }
1723	                        }
1724	                        return '';
1725	                    });
1726	            }
1727	            """,
1728	            vector_id,
1729	        )
1730	        # Fallback: read the result JSON directly from disk for a guaranteed valid evidence_id
1731	        if not evidence_id:
1732	            result_path = Path("outputs/polaris_graph") / f"{vector_id}.json"
1733	            if result_path.exists():
1734	                try:
1735	                    import json as _json
1736	                    with open(result_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as _rf:
1737	                        _rdata = _json.load(_rf)
1738	                    ev_pool = _rdata.get("evidence", [])
1739	                    if ev_pool:
1740	                        evidence_id = ev_pool[0].get("evidence_id", "")
1741	                except Exception:
1742	                    pass
1743	        if evidence_id:
1744	            body = await page.evaluate(
1745	                """
1746	                (url) => fetch(url).then(r => r.ok ? r.json() : {error: r.status}).catch(e => ({error: e.message}))
1747	                """,
1748	                f"/api/research/source-preview/{vector_id}/{evidence_id}",
1749	            )
1750	            if body and not body.get("error"):
1751	                has_preview = body.get("has_preview", False)
1752	                html_len = len(body.get("readability_html", "") or "")
1753	                q72.set_pass(
1754	                    actual=f"has_preview={has_preview}, html={html_len} chars",
1755	                    expected="200 with has_preview",
1756	                )
1757	            else:
1758	                q72.set_fail(actual=body, expected="200 with has_preview", error=f"API error: {body}")
1759	        else:
1760	            q72.set_info(actual="no evidence_id found", note="Cannot test: no evidence ID available")
1761	    else:
1762	        q72.set_info(actual=f"vectorId='{vector_id}'", note="Cannot test: no valid vectorId")
1763	    results.append(q72)
1764	
1765	    return results

1769	# Category R: Visual & Responsive (R73-R77)
1770	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1771	async def run_visual_checks(
1772	    page: Page,
1773	    console_errors: list,
1774	    output_dir: Path,
1775	) -> tuple[list[CheckResult], list[str]]:
1776	    """R73-R77: Screenshots, console errors, broken images, stylesheets."""
1777	    results: list[CheckResult] = []
1778	    screenshots: list[str] = []
1779	    screenshots_dir = output_dir / "screenshots"
1780	    screenshots_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1781	
1782	    # R73: Full-page screenshot at 1920x1080
1783	    r73 = CheckResult("R73", "Full-page screenshot captured at 1920x1080", "R. Visual & Responsive")
1784	    try:
1785	        full_path = screenshots_dir / "full_page_1920x1080.png"
1786	        await page.screenshot(path=str(full_path), full_page=True)
1787	        screenshots.append(str(full_path))
1788	        r73.set_pass(actual=str(full_path), expected="screenshot saved")
1789	    except Exception as exc:
1790	        r73.set_fail(actual=None, expected="screenshot saved", error=str(exc))
1791	    results.append(r73)
1792	
1793	    # R74: Per-view screenshots
1794	    r74 = CheckResult("R74", "Screenshots captured for all 7 views", "R. Visual & Responsive")
1795	    await switch_to_operator_mode(page)
1796	    view_shots: list[str] = []
1797	    failed_shots: list[str] = []
1798	    for view_name in ALL_VIEWS:
1799	        try:
1800	            await switch_view(page, view_name)
1801	            await page.wait_for_timeout(300)
1802	            shot_path = await capture_view_screenshot(page, view_name, output_dir)
1803	            view_shots.append(shot_path)
1804	            screenshots.append(shot_path)
1805	        except Exception as exc:
1806	            failed_shots.append(f"{view_name}: {exc}")
1807	    if not failed_shots:
1808	        r74.set_pass(actual=f"{len(view_shots)} view screenshots", expected="7 screenshots")
1809	    else:
1810	        r74.set_fail(
1811	            actual=f"{len(view_shots)} ok, {len(failed_shots)} failed",
1812	            expected="7 screenshots",
1813	            error=f"Failed: {failed_shots}",
1814	        )
1815	    results.append(r74)
1816	
1817	    # R75: No console errors (filter known non-critical)
1818	    r75 = CheckResult("R75", "No console errors (excluding known non-critical)", "R. Visual & Responsive")
1819	    known_non_critical = ["favicon", "font", "manifest", ".ico", "service-worker", "srcdoc", "sandbox", "failed to load resource"]
1820	    critical_errors: list[str] = []
1821	    for err in console_errors:
1822	        err_text = str(err.text) if hasattr(err, "text") else str(err)
1823	        is_known = any(pattern in err_text.lower() for pattern in known_non_critical)
1824	        if not is_known:
1825	            critical_errors.append(err_text[:200])
1826	    if not critical_errors:
1827	        r75.set_pass(
1828	            actual=f"{len(console_errors)} total, 0 critical",
1829	            expected="0 critical errors",
1830	        )
1831	    else:
1832	        r75.set_fail(
1833	            actual=f"{len(critical_errors)} critical errors",
1834	            expected="0 critical errors",
1835	            error="; ".join(critical_errors[:5]),
1836	        )
1837	    results.append(r75)
1838	
1839	    # R76: No broken LOCAL images (exclude external favicons/CDN images)
1840	    r76 = CheckResult("R76", "No broken local images on page", "R. Visual & Responsive")
1841	    broken_info = await page.evaluate(
1842	        """
1843	        () => {
1844	            const broken = Array.from(document.images).filter(i => {
1845	                if (!i.src) return false;
1846	                // Only check local images — external favicons/CDN often fail in sandboxed tests
1847	                const isLocal = i.src.startsWith(window.location.origin) || i.src.startsWith('/');
1848	                return isLocal && (!i.complete || i.naturalWidth === 0);
1849	            });
1850	            return { count: broken.length, srcs: broken.slice(0, 5).map(i => i.src) };
1851	        }
1852	        """
1853	    )
1854	    broken_local = broken_info["count"]
1855	    if broken_local == 0:
1856	        r76.set_pass(actual=0, expected=0)
1857	    else:
1858	        r76.set_fail(
1859	            actual=broken_local,
1860	            expected=0,
1861	            error=f"{broken_local} broken local images: {broken_info.get('srcs', [])}",
1862	        )
1863	    results.append(r76)
1864	
1865	    # R77: Local stylesheets loaded (exclude cross-origin sheets — CORS blocks cssRules)
1866	    r77 = CheckResult("R77", "Local stylesheets loaded without errors", "R. Visual & Responsive")
1867	    failed_sheets = await page.evaluate(
1868	        """
1869	        () => {
1870	            let failCount = 0;
1871	            for (const s of document.styleSheets) {
1872	                // Cross-origin stylesheets (Google Fonts, CDN) throw SecurityError on cssRules
1873	                // Only count local stylesheets as failures
1874	                if (s.href && !s.href.startsWith(window.location.origin)) continue;
1875	                try { s.cssRules; } catch(e) { failCount++; }
1876	            }
1877	            return failCount;
1878	        }
1879	        """
1880	    )
1881	    if failed_sheets == 0:
1882	        r77.set_pass(actual=0, expected=0)
1883	    else:
1884	        r77.set_fail(actual=failed_sheets, expected=0, error=f"{failed_sheets} local stylesheets failed to load")
1885	    results.append(r77)
1886	
1887	    return results, screenshots

1924	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1925	# Helpers
1926	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1927	def _parse_hms_to_seconds(hms: str) -> int:
1928	    """Parse HH:MM:SS string to total seconds. Returns 0 on failure."""
1929	    match = re.match(r"(\d{2}):(\d{2}):(\d{2})", hms)
1930	    if not match:
1931	        return 0
1932	    hours, minutes, seconds = int(match.group(1)), int(match.group(2)), int(match.group(3))
1933	    return hours * 3600 + minutes * 60 + seconds

1936	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1937	# Report generation
1938	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1939	def generate_report(
1940	    results: list[CheckResult],
1941	    screenshots: list[str],
1942	    console_errors: list,
1943	    output_dir: Path,
1944	) -> dict:
1945	    """Build and write the JSON audit report."""
1946	    by_category: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
1947	    for r in results:
1948	        by_category.setdefault(r.category, []).append(r.to_dict())
1949	
1950	    total = len(results)
1951	    passed = sum(1 for r in results if r.passed)
1952	    failed = total - passed
1953	    info_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.severity == "info")
1954	
1955	    report = {
1956	        "audit": "polaris_fire_test",
1957	        "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
1958	        "summary": {
1959	            "total_checks": total,
1960	            "passed": passed,
1961	            "failed": failed,
1962	            "info": info_count,
1963	            "pass_rate": f"{(passed / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0:.1f}%",
1964	        },
1965	        "categories": by_category,
1966	        "console_errors": [
1967	            str(e.text) if hasattr(e, "text") else str(e) for e in console_errors
1968	        ],
1969	        "screenshots": screenshots,
1970	    }
1971	
1972	    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1973	    report_path = output_dir / "audit_report.json"
1974	    with open(report_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
1975	        json.dump(report, fh, indent=2, default=str)
1976	    logger.info("Report written: %s", report_path)
1977	
1978	    return report
1979	
1980	
1981	def print_console_summary(report: dict) -> None:
1982	    """Print a human-readable PASS/FAIL summary to stdout."""
1983	    summary = report["summary"]
1984	    print("\n" + "=" * 72)
1985	    print("  POLARIS FIRE TEST -- EXHAUSTIVE UI AUDIT (79 checks / 19 categories)")
1986	    print("=" * 72)
1987	    print(f"  Total checks: {summary['total_checks']}")
1988	    print(f"  Passed:       {summary['passed']}")
1989	    print(f"  Failed:       {summary['failed']}")
1990	    print(f"  Info:         {summary['info']}")
1991	    print(f"  Pass rate:    {summary['pass_rate']}")
1992	    print("-" * 72)
1993	
1994	    for category_name, checks in report["categories"].items():
1995	        print(f"\n  {category_name}:")
1996	        for check in checks:
1997	            if check["severity"] == "info":
1998	                marker = "[INFO]"
1999	            elif check["passed"]:
2000	                marker = "[PASS]"
2001	            else:
2002	                marker = "[FAIL]"
2003	            line = f"    {marker} {check['check_id']}: {check['description']}"
2004	            if not check["passed"] and check.get("error") and check["severity"] != "info":
2005	                line += f"  -- {check['error']}"
2006	            elif check["severity"] == "info" and check.get("error"):
2007	                line += f"  -- {check['error']}"
2008	            print(line)
2009	
2010	    if report.get("console_errors"):
2011	        print(f"\n  Console Errors ({len(report['console_errors'])}):")
2012	        for err in report["console_errors"][:10]:
2013	            print(f"    - {err[:120]}")
2014	
2015	    if report.get("screenshots"):
2016	        print(f"\n  Screenshots ({len(report['screenshots'])}):")
2017	        for path in report["screenshots"]:
2018	            print(f"    - {path}")
2019	
2020	    print("=" * 72 + "\n")
